name: Jackson McMillan
subject: We've lost our race (FL06 Special)
body:
| |
---
(I believe we’ve found one) According to ‘conventional wisdom ‘and the ‘very smart consultants’ in DC, this race is already lost. However the facts on the ground point to something different. | [View in browser](https://yqlzye.clicks.mlsend.com/td/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwODE2MzUsXCJsXCI6MTQ5MDc1NjQ2NjU1NDk3MjQ5LFwiclwiOjE0OTA3NTY5NjI3MDk2ODUxOX0iLCJzIjoiYWI0ZjFiYmE5NjY5ZjQwMCJ9)
| |
---
| [ ](https://yqlzye.clicks.mlsend.com/td/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwODE2MzUsXCJsXCI6MTQ5MDc1NjQ2NjU5NjkxNTU0LFwiclwiOjE0OTA3NTY5NjI3MDk2ODUxOX0iLCJzIjoiOThiODJiYmNlNGQ3MjFiYiJ9)
---
|
---
Friend,

I am going to shoot straight with you.

According to the ‘very smart consultants’ in DC, our Democratic campaign in the special election Florida’s 6th Congressional district has **_already lost_** this election _._ They say:
* The district is too red and that Trump/MAGA are too powerful, triumphant, and near omnipresent.
* The narrow pathway to retaking the House via winning the three special elections in 2025 is impossible. Democrat’s only shot of retaking the house is in 2026.
* It would take a _literal miracle_ for us to win here.

When Democrats don’t try we lose. Despite what the institutional losers in DC say (yes the same ones who played a decisive role in our 2024 loss) this race is beyond winnable.

Seeing is believing, and over the past two and a half months, I have seen with my very eyes the rise and rapid scaling of a campaign that could win this.

In these past two months we’ve raised over _$7.3 million_ (what most representatives raise in a two year cycle), have recruited tens of thousands of volunteers in district and from all fifty states, have hired a team of _over a hundred_ _paid staff_ the majority being canvassers who are knocking over _10,000 doors_ a day.

**Even if they were right that a literal miracle would be required to win, I believe we are on the cusp of one.

**_Miracles are happening every day for us._ Just last Friday we were more than $53k off from our vital end of month target, donations were rapidly slowing down, and less than three hours remained on the clock. Not hitting our target and being unable to pay out our massive payroll.

| | [ ](https://yqlzye.clicks.mlsend.com/td/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwODE2MzUsXCJsXCI6MTQ5MDc1NjQ2NjYyODM3MjgzLFwiclwiOjE0OTA3NTY5NjI3MDk2ODUxOX0iLCJzIjoiNGQ5YzEyNzZjY2E1ZTVjNCJ9)
---
|
---
_Screenshot of where we stood on ActBlue last Friday night after my desperate call for support._

|
---
I sent out an emergency request for aid, and our supporters delivered a small miracle (see above). But faith alone and our past achievements will not yield the miracle we need here.

_Will_ is what brings miracles to life. Collectively hundreds of thousands of your peers from every corner of this nation are making it happen. You are flying in to knock doors, phone banking, sending postcards, __donating__ , and more. That work is what will materialize the miracle in Florida’s 6th Congressional District.****

|
---
**[You have the power to will a miracle into existence. Will you make a donation of anything you can spare to usher in a miracle, take back the house in 2025, and set the tone for the next four years with a defiant shot across the bow?](https://yqlzye.clicks.mlsend.com/td/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwODE2MzUsXCJsXCI6MTQ5MDc1NjQ2NjY4MDgwMTY0LFwiclwiOjE0OTA3NTY5NjI3MDk2ODUxOX0iLCJzIjoiZjUyZmUxZmZiYzRmZTg2NyJ9)**
|
---
_If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately._
|

name: Steve Simon
subject: If we don’t act now…

**Closing the curtains on my campaign.**

I'm at my wits' end, Friend.

The Democrats just have too much power, too many connections, and too much in their war chest.

Now they're raising $50 MILLION to flip my seat blue.

In 2024, I was outspent 3 to 1 by the Democrats trying to buy my seat and DC Democrat machine has already pledged even more against my reelection.

At this point, if I don't do a total 180 on the fundraising front, not only will we lose my House seat, Republicans WILL lose the House. It'll be Democrats impeaching Trump and stonewalling the AMERICA FIRST agenda all over again.
**[Friend, this is Ryan Zinke. I served as President Trump's first Secretary of the Interior and I was a ground forces commander at SEAL Team 6.](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=0b79a6ac6880481588bac2183b6d301e&_e=8lWFTMfT17ZXrES39eAem4dUiuLnCTQlWKqU2AtNCO_f9zzV5AYsUY0yWg51wRn2XLT0BOf4kl1SJ-cj_uwIB4SvO1LarirgRNDSa3-jrFY1PIlv7cIHnWXRGslr1os7t5pvFx0uyDGCNvcqjIJHEPAb7pjZ5TtVdz76voU3I6DGGeAl_8pq920sw5tmAw1pA3srZpHoumsqDYxvMf3o-g4-x0SDS8w3AoNtghbO9hI2Sl8mitR2fYnRrZIXp4gBA5k1dN7_o077KZhGWg-STZd8Jh3A1XkFIlWwq2ivp2auySSJq6yYuPs2HMEc5UfBdhwEZeh0IHN-5UqfBXFcl09DLLuxy2IaHf51W_EDUSWG96qCOUbEBUgCHJo75AG_YhnCy_uPYOfdae2krtVfJa3sEhK0LKon0CuuvgnW9k1bihAbUrXSFvNDzKg9PQY3bukdZVaHc-u0PfCgZOy98A%3D%3D "Friend, this is Ryan Zinke. I served as President Trump's first Secretary of the Interior and I was a ground forces commander at SEAL Team 6.")**
_**In 2022, I led the team of Navy SEALs that retook the House Majority, and I have more battle experience than anyone else. Point is, I'm one of the toughest, most battle-tested Republicans in our party.**_

If the Democrats destroy me in this race, I can guarantee you no other Republican will have a chance.

_**[But if you can chip in just $5 right now](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=0b79a6ac6880481588bac2183b6d301e&_e=8lWFTMfT17ZXrES39eAem4dUiuLnCTQlWKqU2AtNCO_f9zzV5AYsUY0yWg51wRn2XLT0BOf4kl1SJ-cj_uwIB4SvO1LarirgRNDSa3-jrFY1PIlv7cIHnWXRGslr1os7t5pvFx0uyDGCNvcqjIJHEPAb7pjZ5TtVdz76voU3I6DGGeAl_8pq920sw5tmAw1pA3srZpHoumsqDYxvMf3o-g4-x0SDS8w3AoNtghbO9hI2Sl8mitR2fYnRrZIXp4gBA5k1dN7_o077KZhGWg-STZd8Jh3A1XkFIlWwq2ivp2auySSJq6yYuPs2HMEc5UfBHCjdrVssL9k6cYtzGct4NVbNZ3QbDXRxRQu3TJ19AabWldnRmqnGpa1AA_YLAQlIdwsRFi1hD9O77kNUn_sLY4XgZW4s0hLBiI0y9uWweR3f6VeqDoJ975DVIDYcWQYyoTWo9Ny6hvXOWEcuJykRQQ%3D%3D "But if you can chip in just $5 right now")**_ , I can promise you that we will blow my fundraising goal out of the water and ruin the Democrats' plan to destroy our nation.

I'm humbly asking you, as a warrior who's fought for this nation my entire life, will you help me out?

_****_

The Democrats just have too much power, too many connections, and too much in their war chest. Now they're raising $50 MILLION to flip my seat blue. In 2024, I was outspent 3 to 1 by the Democrats trying to buy my seat and DC Democrat machine has already pledged even more against my reelection. At this point, if I don't do a total 180 on the fundraising front, not only will we lose my House seat, Republicans WILL lose the House. It'll be Democrats impeaching Trump and stonewalling the AMERICA FIRST agenda all over again. "Friend, this is Ryan Zinke. I served as President Trump's first Secretary of the Interior and I was a ground forces commander at SEAL Team 6." In 2022, I led the team of Navy SEALs that retook the House Majority, and I have more battle experience than anyone else. Point is, I'm one of the toughest, most battle-tested Republicans in our party. If the Democrats destroy me in this race, I can guarantee you no other Republican will have a chance. "But if you can chip in just $5 right now" , I can promise you that we will blow my fundraising goal out of the water and ruin the Democrats' plan to destroy our nation. I'm humbly asking you, as a warrior who's fought for this nation my entire life, will you help me out? | | You have my word, as a former Navy SEAL and commander at SEAL Team 6, I'll always serve you proudly. Thank you for your support, Ryan Zinke | | | | Ryan Zinke is a retired member of the United States Navy. | Hi Friend -
---
I’m writing this from a plane, flying to yet another state that isn’t my own, again putting on my armor to fight back against the relentless lawfare waged against True the Vote—and me personally.
It’s encouraging to see President Trump and his administration scrutinizing law firms that have weaponized the legal system for political attacks. But for us, for now, the battles still rage. We are preparing for hearings and trials throughout the spring and summer.
When I started True the Vote in 2010, I’d never been in court. Since then, I’ve never been out. The years have hardened me.
What keeps me going is ... you.
You, your children, my children, and an unshakable conviction that the sanctity of their future votes must be fought for today.
It’s no fun being drug through the mud in the courtroom or in the press, but it comes with the territory. It’s part of the fight. We know who we are and what we have, and have not, done. We are standing firm.
But, [we need your help](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.bYZLwP2tKNW52PF5H5Fs6UAj3E_p8cNGXIOwo9MdaP4uURHu2CF_xBMsTaXvArHujZCYcWDflXvAHZOdWogeY67YYVzOuRaIaHqb0EQF0i6ti96rrq3S5noXyuTduKaO5Bkpdqg4vBZHFNNd0Bk3uxEYW5BYzho5NlaZE21Iz3IHyAzvPUPjWvaTqIcaKSBX3_uNv5zZTF5A6_nI-nZncPn98Lgc4XXTNDK4P2AXmwfihKz7Toi6XvycKylLtntC0UELu0q2F4fbsCMieH7-OQ/4ev/CnX4C2DyTGSCLfvekQ1slA/h2/h001.bSj5_s-gbtlP3AXI-rLTl_wAfcd-HSKfilmuXTKuX40) to survive the upcoming gauntlet.
Asking for help is not something I do well. I’m asking now. [If you can make a donation today, it would be greatly appreciated. ](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.bYZLwP2tKNW52PF5H5Fs6UAj3E_p8cNGXIOwo9MdaP4uURHu2CF_xBMsTaXvArHujZCYcWDflXvAHZOdWogeY67YYVzOuRaIaHqb0EQF0i6ti96rrq3S5noXyuTduKaO5Bkpdqg4vBZHFNNd0Bk3uxEYW5BYzho5NlaZE21Iz3IHyAzvPUPjWvaTqIcaKSBXdxpPRkcKaNc4RQyRFRpF_PMZlo1-eVb3kwEJDkhLLyVJ1sUNtAQBKIOMSqm6l9yDt2hCSD4EyqU-hWZ0QKhFiA/4ev/CnX4C2DyTGSCLfvekQ1slA/h3/h001.qJz-qqs7d6FGmmcfaKekfjLw_NsJvkE1lhvbCBnIWFM)

clean_body: If elections aren't truly fair, we aren't truly free. | | | March 16, 2025 | | | Hi Friend - I’m writing this from a plane, flying to yet another state that isn’t my own, again putting on my armor to fight back against the relentless lawfare waged against True the Vote—and me personally. It’s encouraging to see President Trump and his administration scrutinizing law firms that have weaponized the legal system for political attacks. But for us, for now, the battles still rage. We are preparing for hearings and trials throughout the spring and summer. When I started True the Vote in 2010, I’d never been in court. Since then, I’ve never been out. The years have hardened me. What keeps me going is ... you. You, your children, my children, and an unshakable conviction that the sanctity of their future votes must be fought for today. It’s no fun being drug through the mud in the courtroom or in the press, but it comes with the territory. It’s part of the fight. We know who we are and what we have, and have not, done. We are standing firm. But, to survive the upcoming gauntlet. Asking for help is not something I do well. I’m asking now. | ) We are a very small organization, up against Leftist juggernauts like Stacey Abrams, Mark Elias, and the vestiges of the Biden DOJ. They have billions at their disposal. We have our wits, our tenacity, and each other. If you make a contribution to the cause, we will put it to immediate use. And whether or not you can support us financially, I humbly ask for your prayers. Ephesians 6. Put on the armor, everyday. Keep the faith. Never quit. Ever onward - | P.S. We’ve had a lot of folks ask us to include a newsletter sign-up link. P.P.S. You’re awesome. America is making a comeback. We can do this. | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- | | Update your email preferences or unsubscribe (C) 2025 True the Vote 3120 Southwest Frwy Ste 101, PMB 19128 Houston, Texas 77098-4520, United States of America

name: Team Mikie
subject: Anything, Peter?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Less than 3 months until Mikie’s election day in New Jersey.
Peter,
We are officially less than 3 months until Election Day in New Jersey, so we have to ask: **[Is there anything we could say to convince you to split a donation of $10 between Heartland Patriots and Mikie’s campaign?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/87063?refcode=em20250316-8823&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8823%2E614464%2Ev_EANf)**
**[DONATE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/87063?refcode=em20250316-8823&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=8823%2E614464%2Ev_EANf)**
What if we told you that we have under three months to rally New Jersey voters around Mikie’s vision for our future?
How about the fact that with Trump in the White House, we need a strong leader to stand up to his administration and fight for working families — and that we can count on Mikie to be that leader?
What if we said that just $10 right now goes a long way in reaching folks across the Garden State with Mikie’s message?
_All of this is true, Peter._
With your help, we can elect Mikie as our next governor. She is ready to take on the toughest fights and win them, just like she has all her life. But with Election Day rapidly approaching and Republicans going all-in to flip New Jersey red, she needs our support ASAP.
**[Peter, will you please split $10 between Mikie’s campaign and Heartland Patriots to help elect Mikie as New Jersey’s next governor?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/87063?refcode=em20250316-8823&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=8823%2E614464%2Ev_EANf)**

name: GOTV PAC
subject: Billionaires are trying to buy this election. Let’s stop them.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email2082025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email2082025&amount=25]
Peter —
Elon Musk and his right-wing allies have poured $10 million into the Wisconsin Supreme Court race because they want to buy control of the courts—just like they’ve bought politicians who do their bidding. They don’t care about democracy. They don’t care about working people. They care about protecting their wealth and their power.
But here’s the good news: they can spend all the money in the world on nasty ads, but they can’t buy turnout.
That is where we come in. GOTV PAC runs one of the most effective grassroots voter turnout programs in the country. While the corporate-backed groups waste millions on consultants and TV ads, we invest in real voter contact.
Right now, we are running a paid phonebank operation to turn out voters for the April 1st Wisconsin Supreme Court election—a race that will determine the future of abortion rights, fair maps, and our ability to hold Donald Trump accountable.
So here is the question: Will we let Elon Musk and the billionaire class buy this election? Or will we fight back with people power? Because we run our phone bank operations in-house, every $10 donation we receive allows us to reach one voter! Significantly less than the industry average of $75— making your donation go further! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email3162025&amount=10]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=Email3162025] DONATE $35 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?express_lane=true&amount=35&refcode=Email3162025]
DONATE $60 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?express_lane=true&amount=60&refcode=Email3162025] DONATE $120 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?express_lane=true&amount=120&refcode=Email3162025]
OTHER AMOUNT → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email3162025]
This is how we win tough elections—by doing the hard work of organizing, talking to voters, and turning them out. We don’t take shortcuts. We don’t sell out to big donors. We do the work.
And with your help, we will win.
GOTV PAC
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email3162025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email3162025&amount=25]
Get Out the Vote PAC mobilizes voters often overlooked by mainstream campaigns to help Democratic candidates win in the most competitive districts.
Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member.
Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]
PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
GOTV PAC
712 H St NE
PMB 96591
Washington, DC 20002
United States
Get Out the Vote PAC is registered with the Federal Election Commission as a hybrid Political Action Committee (PAC) with FEC ID C00839407. Our mission is to Flip the House by supporting targeted Congressional districts and down-ballot races within those districts. Our internal research evidences that the impact of a Dollar invested in this manner amplifies the top and bottom of the ticket.

body:
Personally asking.
Wow— _I am so grateful, Peter_.

Since I reached out yesterday, the response has been incredible.

Thanks to the support of this grassroots team, we’re so close to hitting our $10,000 mid-month fundraising goal for March!

Now, we just need one final push from this team to ensure we're in a good spot as we prepare to post strong numbers for the first time this year.

This is our chance to show that WA-08 is already in the fight to keep me representing you in Congress. **I'm counting on just 47 more donors to step up and chip in before midnight tonight. Will you be one of them? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/schrier-march-25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103977644/533902337/-1764777207?refcode=em25_250316_mm3_fndd_gq_act___ksper_ettx&&&&utm_campaign=em25_250316_mm3_fndd_gq_act___ksper_ettx&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&refcode2=8f7d2471-d102-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&refcode2=8f7d2471-d102-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjdkMjQ3MS1kMTAyLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ir3VP2UeT8C2nBs9_uq5tnZqhDV3AANv-NSh6sHCOQs=)**

# [Practicing What I Preach](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=159222610&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU5MjIyNjEwLCJpYXQiOjE3NDIxNzY4NDAsImV4cCI6MTc0NDc2ODg0MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.0KI_2Cvw4FoylSHtWWOBJ77gCN82RG4CD7QAPTRSQAo)
### How a Generalist Ended Up Making Pizza
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---
| Mar 17
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=159222610&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU5MjIyNjEwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0MjE3Njg0MCwiZXhwIjoxNzQ0NzY4ODQwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTY2MTk5OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.buBAKqHh-l81VSP9edfBUNtLDe4ZBSwJsSx2R7UTYIs&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/af4a4fe0-0b37-4fb4-befc-390460aee67b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
For years, I've suggested that the best way to navigate uncertainty is to integrate thinking by crossing silos and connecting dots. I've often described this method as the "generalist approach" because it prioritizes breadth of perspective over depth of expertise. It is impossible, I believe, to fully appreciate the complexities of our time without combining geopolitical, macroeconomic, regulatory, political, technological, demographic, and social perspectives. At the core of my thinking is a simple belief: _**context matters**_.
And so when I decided to return to the business world last fall, I started practicing what I preached. As I evaluated the many start-up, acquisition, and investment ideas that were swirling around my head, I constantly returned to my analytical framework of trying to understand the context. How, for instance, might a warmer planet impact the demand for HVAC services? Sure, demand for air conditioning might rise, but what about heating? Or how could demographics affect a sawmill's potential revenue streams? Would aging populations help or hurt the market for building materials? And regardless of industry, I always returned to asking myself about technology. How might technology disrupt the industry, sector, or business I was analyzing?
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..q5Iiyv_xeDxQ10MRZUl6PjtoeaBK1jAjFeDSzvm0kTI?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=159222610)
I evaluated dozens of potential opportunities before homing in on one opportunity that caught my attention. During the summer of 2024, a New Hampshire-based pizza manufacturing operation had suddenly shut its doors, sending several dozen employees to the unemployment lines. Attempting to salvage value, banks and others who lent money to the company took control and began a process to find a buyer for the business. When I learned about the situation, there were several interested bidders looking to buy and restore the company. It wasn't until those parties failed to make a deal that I began to really dig into the opportunity of entering the pizza manufacturing business.
I began my diligence by focusing on the context. Regardless of the unique circumstances of the specific opportunity, I had to answer a simple question: Do I even want to be in the frozen pizza business?
**Do I even want to be in the frozen pizza business?**
To answer this question, I started my analysis with a focus on the big picture and the trends that were impacting the business environment. Three major pressures quickly emerged as critical to appreciate.
1. First, rising global **protectionism** seemed destined to intensify. The announcement by the Trump Administration to form an "External Revenue Service" suggested a wholesale rethinking of government finances was underway. In the short run, this seemed likely to generate higher prices and persistent **inflation** - a _de facto_ regressive tax that would disproportionately hurt the working class and those on fixed incomes.
2. Second, I noticed that the rise of **weight loss drugs** such as Ozempic had Americans paying more attention to what - and how much - they were putting on their plates and in their mouths. **Portion sizes** were shrinking.
3. And third, the elevation of Robert F. Kennedy Jr and the "**Make America Healthy Again** " movement pointed to a move away from highly processed foods containing artificial ingredients and chemical preservatives.

After identifying the pressures, I turned to understanding how they each impacted the pizza opportunity I was considering. To understand the impact of protectionism and inflation, I began with a look into the pizza supply chain, which is mostly sourced from America. (Sure, there are small items such as basil that are imported, or fiber for packaging, but the big inputs are almost all sourced domestically). While supply chain disruption is always possible for manufacturing businesses, a global trade war didn't seem to produce an existential risk.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..q5Iiyv_xeDxQ10MRZUl6PjtoeaBK1jAjFeDSzvm0kTI?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=159222610)
Further, because inflation pinched consumers and left them with less purchasing power, they _tended to consume more, not fewer_ , low-priced items such as frozen pizza. Instead of eating out, lots of people were opting to put a frozen pizza in the oven. And when consumers feel poor, they tend to buy store brands rather than more expensive national brands. The data was convincing. Net-net, protectionism, tariffs, and inflation seemed to help the frozen pizza business.
Next, I turned to the Ozempic factor, which was a headwind that I initially assumed would hurt demand for pizza. But within the frozen pizza market, which appears stagnant at the aggregate level, there is an interesting shift taking place. Demand for single-serve pizzas is growing at the expense of larger, full-size pizzas. Yes, total calories might be going down, but the single-serve pizza market is booming!
And finally, the RFK factor led me to explore how pizza as a category stood up against alternatives...and in this regard, frozen pizza was far more insulated than low-priced but highly processed and chemically preserved foods. On the margin, pizza might be a beneficiary of these shifting trends.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/15e724c2-a071-45c0-b87c-a78b0fa07439?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: [https://www.fastcompany.com/91232836/these-pro-rfk-jr-influencers-are-pushing-to-make-america-healthy-again](https://substack.com/redirect/78822d70-5a8f-4fb6-801d-7ec0740bcae3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Armed with this understanding (right or wrong, it was _**my**_ understanding) of the context, I decided to pursue a purchase of _only a portion_ of the business, specifically the assets related to the private label business. In times of inflation, as I thought were likely in the short run, consumers would migrate towards frozen pizza and would likely choose the store brands. In times of deflation (likely caused by a bad economy), I realized, the consumer would likely do the same. _**It appeared that demand for private label frozen pizza would be strong in almost any business environment.**_ The conclusion from the weight-loss drug analysis informed my post-acquisition strategy. We would disproportionately focus on the single-serve market. And lastly, the fact that frozen pizza can be made with cleaner ingredients than most other similarly-priced options suggested it was insulated from "RFK Risk" and might even benefit from shifting demand.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3717fad7-4f03-46b2-96c0-d1740511b52e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**The Jetsons ' Food-O-Matic**. Source: [https://www.mccormick.northwestern.edu/engineering-management/inside-our-program/stories/2015/we-finally-have-the-jetsons-food.html](https://substack.com/redirect/9bf82f99-10c9-4fc7-aa1b-a77486432a18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
As I mentioned above, I also wanted to consider how technology might disrupt the frozen pizza business. While I could imagine ways in which technology and artificial intelligence could increase manufacturing productivity or even help generate new and novel recipes, I had trouble thinking about ways in which technology led people to either consume less pizza or eliminate it from their diet entirely. Perhaps a "food-o-matic" machine like the Jetson's used? ([It's not as crazy as it sounds](https://substack.com/redirect/9bf82f99-10c9-4fc7-aa1b-a77486432a18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)
After an intense period of negotiations with multiple financial institutions, I was able to acquire the assets I sought at an extremely attractive price. And in early December, [Goodwell Foods](https://substack.com/redirect/37325457-0931-4e54-a724-6c3e01a97c76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a **private label** frozen pizza manufacturer located in Pittsfield, NH, began operations. We now employ several dozen people, and using fresh, **natural ingredients** , our state-of-the-art production line is achieving record productivity. We are disproportionately focused on **single-serve** frozen pizzas and plan to develop allergy-friendly products such as gluten-free and dairy-free pizzas, among other options, in the future.
Faith in the generalist approach led me to the industry and is already helping me navigate the overwhelming uncertainty. And that's how a generalist like me ended up making pizza.

---|---|---
**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/73f158a1-c2f4-4603-a442-99a8cc4ee960?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/91216699-39c8-4512-9cb3-2a14428c1160?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/8337db82-c4ea-4643-ae45-2ee94bf14fe9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.
You're currently a free subscriber to [Navigating Uncertainty](https://substack.com/redirect/9c4ff9aa-9906-4abf-aa82-b548240eaef4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Kennedy Jr and the "Make America Healthy Again " movement pointed to a move away from highly processed foods containing artificial ingredients and chemical preservatives. To understand the impact of protectionism and inflation, I began with a look into the pizza supply chain, which is mostly sourced from America. (Sure, there are small items such as basil that are imported, or fiber for packaging, but the big inputs are almost all sourced domestically). While supply chain disruption is always possible for manufacturing businesses, a global trade war didn't seem to produce an existential risk. Further, because inflation pinched consumers and left them with less purchasing power, they tended to consume more, not fewer , low-priced items such as frozen pizza. Instead of eating out, lots of people were opting to put a frozen pizza in the oven. And when consumers feel poor, they tend to buy store brands rather than more expensive national brands. The data was convincing. Net-net, protectionism, tariffs, and inflation seemed to help the frozen pizza business. Next, I turned to the Ozempic factor, which was a headwind that I initially assumed would hurt demand for pizza. But within the frozen pizza market, which appears stagnant at the aggregate level, there is an interesting shift taking place. Demand for single-serve pizzas is growing at the expense of larger, full-size pizzas. Yes, total calories might be going down, but the single-serve pizza market is booming! And finally, the RFK factor led me to explore how pizza as a category stood up against alternatives...and in this regard, frozen pizza was far more insulated than low-priced but highly processed and chemically preserved foods. On the margin, pizza might be a beneficiary of these shifting trends. | | ---|---|--- Source: Armed with this understanding (right or wrong, it was my understanding) of the context, I decided to pursue a purchase of only a portion of the business, specifically the assets related to the private label business. In times of inflation, as I thought were likely in the short run, consumers would migrate towards frozen pizza and would likely choose the store brands. In times of deflation (likely caused by a bad economy), I realized, the consumer would likely do the same. It appeared that demand for private label frozen pizza would be strong in almost any business environment. The conclusion from the weight-loss drug analysis informed my post-acquisition strategy. We would disproportionately focus on the single-serve market. And lastly, the fact that frozen pizza can be made with cleaner ingredients than most other similarly-priced options suggested it was insulated from "RFK Risk" and might even benefit from shifting demand. | | ---|---|--- The Jetsons ' Food-O-Matic. Source: As I mentioned above, I also wanted to consider how technology might disrupt the frozen pizza business. While I could imagine ways in which technology and artificial intelligence could increase manufacturing productivity or even help generate new and novel recipes, I had trouble thinking about ways in which technology led people to either consume less pizza or eliminate it from their diet entirely. Perhaps a "food-o-matic" machine like the Jetson's used? (.) After an intense period of negotiations with multiple financial institutions, I was able to acquire the assets I sought at an extremely attractive price. And in early December, , a private label frozen pizza manufacturer located in Pittsfield, NH, began operations. We now employ several dozen people, and using fresh, natural ingredients , our state-of-the-art production line is achieving record productivity. We are disproportionately focused on single-serve frozen pizzas and plan to develop allergy-friendly products such as gluten-free and dairy-free pizzas, among other options, in the future. Faith in the generalist approach led me to the industry and is already helping me navigate the overwhelming uncertainty. And that's how a generalist like me ended up making pizza. | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and . Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Kelan Publishing, LLC PO BOX 932, Lincoln, NH 03251 ? ?

It’s clear that people are frustrated by Trump’s attack on our democracy and refusal to take action to address the high cost of living. But state Democrats are getting the job done. | | |
---
| |
---|---
Last week, we asked our grassroots donors how the Trump administration is impacting them. Here’s what we heard: **→** You said you’re feeling **"stress and fear for the loss of my financial independence."** **→** You shared you’re feeling **"disheartened and absolutely furious about the War on Women!"** **→** You noted that **"everything has gone up in price"** **And almost half of all our grassroots donors said “Threats to Democracy” was their biggest concern.** These are real responses from folks around the country, but we haven’t heard from you yet – it’s not too late to share your input. [ Will you take a few minutes to complete our survey on how the Trump administration impacts you? ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hWzRBi1I12trvGpCMAmsq3z5ysu48YH6XY3aVhlMqLYTf3pVp530N7dAoVJ53FHZETDFkWn1sKIDOZx59rFloyhwpav9U3O4Otkiyvdk1kGZS3KWPeP05toElojK9awGdLxy4T9XDLjmJwzXVStVO2SbIN3r20Rb_jQk1O7rrII-iOUIPtIWBZBnHkR-69GEWBkKiCb-3VTinrmCkT9wcccWsDPdbqK4RklxqGp28rWoah7zXDkY2tYGEbmlK_ilC0QZ3fTqOEiM6cc7eSnvUuekJ-tn81qb2_-h22Hmycse2GymkveyCcKnrhdDo2-AMekaPIXb4sf48j2xegagWvI1LwSrHluR3KM7AZSfyahM0dwoIdMIvoNx3g-T_dmxRRgbmgCosaZfhPt2UbMLIyrAdPb_Z_wPiY5mTAbGFpuPgIbJ3rsaXaDnqJzKcO6cbxcz6ypLaigJPdYqgEHN-oGpVzrsRUXc7T6kt08fjcR0Qr_dgG_kTT02wDqg5QVjSkg/4ev/ikKPX_8jSqySj6fmGi-oYg/h0/h001.x2gWbd35BIYbicsVnFkw3_8VrxhAhapC-6mvp2y_DYE) **The Trump administration is negatively impacting people like you.** We read about family members losing their jobs because of DOGE and about Americans who fear losing Social Security and Medicare. It’s clear that people are frustrated by Trump’s attack on our democracy and refusal to take action to address the high cost of living.**But state Democrats are getting the job done.** Democrats are bringing real solutions to address the affordability crisis that Trump is aggravating. clean_body: It’s clear that people are frustrated by Trump’s attack on our democracy and refusal to take action to address the high cost of living. But state Democrats are getting the job done. | | | | | ---|--- Last week, we asked our grassroots donors how the Trump administration is impacting them. Here’s what we heard: → You said you’re feeling "stress and fear for the loss of my financial independence." → You shared you’re feeling "disheartened and absolutely furious about the War on Women!" → You noted that "everything has gone up in price" And almost half of all our grassroots donors said “Threats to Democracy” was their biggest concern. These are real responses from folks around the country, but we haven’t heard from you yet – it’s not too late to share your input. The Trump administration is negatively impacting people like you. We read about family members losing their jobs because of DOGE and about Americans who fear losing Social Security and Medicare. It’s clear that people are frustrated by Trump’s attack on our democracy and refusal to take action to address the high cost of living.But state Democrats are getting the job done. Democrats are bringing real solutions to address the affordability crisis that Trump is aggravating. We’ve passed legislation to eliminate the grocery tax in Illinois, increase the minimum wage in Delaware, provide paid family and medical leave in Maine, and more. name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 3/16/2025
body:
The Battle for Colorado’s Future – GOP Chair Race, 2026 Elections & What’s Next
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**March 16, 2025** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST** **By Heidi Ganahl**
| | Dear Readers,
In today's Sunday News Digest, I am giving you an inside look into my newest episode of _Unleashed with Heidi Ganahl!_ You can [listen here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
With just under two weeks until the March 29 Colorado GOP Chair election, the battle for the future of the party is heating up. The next Chair will shape the direction of the party for the next two years, and with the 2026 elections already taking form, the stakes couldn’t be higher.
**GOP Chair Race: The Candidates & Their Plans**
We now have a clearer picture of how some candidates plan to lead. Rocky Mountain Voice sent out a [candidate survey](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), and while some still have not responded, Brita Horn, Darcy Schoening, and Kevin McCarney have [laid out their strategies](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
* [Brita Horn](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) positions herself as a unifier, focused on grassroots engagement, bringing factions together, and restoring trust in the party’s fundraising efforts.
* Darcy Schoening wants to build a winning machine by securing donors early, investing in local races, and aggressively closing primaries.
* Kevin McCarney is pushing a “Colorado First” approach, rebuilding the Trump coalition, and keeping the GOP primary open to unaffiliated voters.

A key issue at play? How we nominate our candidates as a party. First, [Proposition 108.](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Some candidates want to overturn it and return to a party-only primary process, while others believe opting out is a losing battle. Second, some candidates want to hold a vote of the State Central Committee to move to a “no ballot primary.” Read the [candidate survey](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) to find out where each stands.
**Legislative Battles: The Good, The Bad & The Ugly**
Over 497 bills have been introduced this session, and the Democratic majority continues its aggressive push on everything from gun control to abortion expansion. Here’s a quick (mini) [breakdown](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.):
✅ The Good: ▪️ [HB25-1040](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Adding [Nuclear Energy as a Clean Energy](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Resource (Passed) ▪️ [HB25-1051](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Repeal Recycled Paper Carryout Bag Fee (Lost) ▪️ [HB25-1068](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Malpractice Insurers Gender-Affirming Care Minors (Lost) ▪️ [HB25-1074](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Change Confinement Standards [Egg-Laying Hens](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) (Lost) ▪️ [HB25-1143](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Open-Source Software in Voting Systems (Lost) ▪️ [HB25-1164](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Constitutional [Carry of Handgun](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) (Lost) ▪️ [SB25-057](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Noncitizen Voter Registration Cancellation (Lost)
❌ The Bad: ▪️ [SB25-003](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Banning [Semiautomatic Firearms](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) & Rapid-Fire Devices (Under Consideration) ▪️ [SB25-005](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Worker Protection [Collective Bargaining](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) (Under Consideration) ▪️ [SB25-045](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Health-Care Payment System Analysis (Under Consideration) ▪️ [SB25-081](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Treasurer’s Office (Under Consideration)
Colorado’s legislature is a runaway train for progressive policies. If conservatives want to stop the tide, grassroots engagement is key. Thank you Kevin Lundberg for the great breakdown on bills [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
**2026 Elections: The Field Takes Shape**
With Governor Jared Polis term-limited, speculation about the Democratic field is in full swing. Senator [Michael Bennet](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and Attorney General [Phil Weiser](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) are eyeing a run, while Secretary of State [Jena Griswold](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) may shift to the AG race, and Congressman Joe Neguse is rumored to be considering it.
On the Republican side, names like Barb Kirkmeyer and Joe Oltmann are being floated, while Jason Mikesell, Scott Bottoms and Mark Baisley have announced - but it’s still wide open. Congressional races in CD3 (Jeff Hurd) and CD8 (Gabe Evans) will also be crucial battlegrounds.
**Call to Action: Be the Leader in Your Community**
Now more than ever, we need grassroots action. As I shared on _[Unleashed](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_ , leadership starts in your neighborhood. Become a precinct leader. Attend school board and city council meetings. Write for Rocky Mountain Voice. This is how we take Colorado back.
Let’s build a movement that can win. Stay loud, stay engaged, and stay in the fight.
In liberty and truth,
Heidi
---
|
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[Anderson: SB25-003 faces Constitutional hurdles under the Bruen standard](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Many conservatives and Second Amendment advocates have raised concerns about the constitutionality of SB25-003, a bill that seeks to make it significantly more difficult for Coloradans to exercise their Second Amendment rights.
**[House Democrats push “most outrageous” SB25-003 forward after 12-hour hearing](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The sheriff in town may be tasked with the administrative burden of weighing in on your future firearm purchase, if Democrat lawmakers have their say.
**[CO AG Weiser on DOJ’s review of Tina Peters’ case: “Grotesque attempt to weaponize the rule of law”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The DOJ is now reviewing whether federal agencies engaged in politically motivated lawfare in the high-profile case of former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters. In response to the DOJ’s filing, Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser urged the Denver federal court to reject its interference.
|
---
**LEGISLATIVE SESSION**
**[HB 1010 an attempt to expand governor’s power, Rep. Hartsook complains](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) State Rep. Anthony Hartsook is concerned with the tsunami of bills he sees filtering through the legislature. “Numerous bills expanding government control continue to flood the General Assembly, threatening businesses and economic freedom,” he wrote in a report to his District 44 constituency.

**[Who gets to know? Lawmakers, media and watchdogs wrangle over public records access](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Last year Democrat lawmakers created a loophole to dodge public scrutiny—and Governor Polis made it law. Now it’s easier for lawmakers to evade state open meetings law, through written and electronic communication.

**[Colorado fuel retailers face up to $20,000 fine for failing to post warning stickers about global warming under proposed bill](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) According to a proposal by Democrats at the Colorado state Capitol, up to a $20,000 fine will hang over the heads of every retailer who sells either liquid or gas fuels if they don’t have global warming warning stickers on their pumps and products if House Bill 25-1277 is signed into law.

**[Universal preschool bill dies as some Colorado programs still complain of bumps](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE COLORADO SUN](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Colorado’s $344 million universal preschool program is popular among families, but some providers say they’re still wrestling with problems that make it hard for families to secure seats or for preschools to sustain themselves financially.

**[House Bill 1208: Price controls for a minimum wage mistake](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[COMPLETE COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) What happens when the price of eggs soars? People buy fewer eggs and start looking for substitutes. What would happen if, say, government set a $10 minimum price on a dozen eggs, higher than the usual price in stores these days?

_**[READ MORE LEGISLATIVE SESSION UPDATES](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**STATE**
**[DOGE is terminating $9.4M in leases across Colorado](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[FOX 31 NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Government agencies nationwide are experiencing spending cuts and layoffs with billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, and leases for property across Colorado are on the list.

**[Attorneys general announce final plan to end Google’s search engine monopoly](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE CENTER SQUARE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser announced a coalition of 38 attorneys general and the Department of Justice have proposed a final package of remedies to end Google’s monopoly over internet search engines.

**[Health care costs spike for undocumented immigrants](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[AXIOS DENVER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A Colorado program that provides taxpayer-funded health care to unauthorized immigrants is seeing costs spike more than 600% after the latest influx.

_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[Congressman Gabe Evans introduces BELOS Act to support military families](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) In a move to honor America’s fallen service members and their families, Colorado Congressman Gabe Evans has introduced the Benefits that Endure for Lifetimes of Service (BELOS) Act, a bipartisan bill that would provide free lifetime National Park passes to families of veterans who passed away due to service-related illness or injury.
**[Department Of Education cuts staff by 50 percent](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE FEDERALIST](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The U.S. Department of Education announced on Tuesday evening that it will be cutting its staff by about 50 percent as part of a “reduction-in-force” to start downsizing the department.

**[Trump says US will experience ‘period of transition’ when asked if economy could see a recession this year](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [FOX BUSINESS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) President Donald Trump declined to explicitly rule out a full-blown recession for the U.S. economy this year, telling Maria Bartiromo in a recent “Sunday Morning Futures” exclusive that the country will see a “period of transition” as his policies take effect.

**[Marco Rubio cancels 83% of USAID programs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[BREITBART](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Eighty-three percent of the programs directly funded and administered by the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) were canceled Monday by Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

**_[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Rio Blanco County hires deputy trained to investigate livestock depredation](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Responding to concerns of livestock growers and community members, and to assist in investigating livestock depredations that occur in Rio Blanco County, commissioners have authorized the sheriff’s office to hire their first Animal and Livestock Conflict Investigator.

**[Taxed and voiceless: How a debt trap turned Meadows Metro District residents into an ATM for bondholders](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Imagine paying a $200 tax each month for a community infrastructure project that was completed decades ago. To add insult to injury, consider being told that you’ll continue doing so for 100 years.

**[Mesa County Commissioner advocates for fair share of transportation dollars](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) With an ever shrinking budget, and an ever increasing demand for state money for road repairs and construction due to increasing population and use on the Western Slope, Mesa County Commissioner, Bobbie Daniels went to the state legislature to put a spotlight on the needs of western Colorado.

**[10 months after hailstorm closed it, Rep. Boebert is demanding reopening of Yuma post office](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) On the Eastern Plains of Colorado, tornadic storms delivering high wind, hail and damage can be commonplace. Property can be damaged by multiple storms a year.

**[Former City Councilman argues for greater transparency in Grand Junction’s election filing process](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Three days. That’s all the time a Grand Junction resident has to challenge a candidate’s petition. But there’s one problem: the public doesn’t even know when the clock starts ticking.

**[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[Ruehmann: NGOs and PACs took Colorado—here’s how we take it back](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _In the early 2000s, Colorado was a bastion of conservative governance, with a Republican majority steering state politics. But by 2012, Democrats had orchestrated a dramatic resurgence._ __
**[Walcher: Zombies That Can Never Be Killed](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _In Haitian folklore a zombie is a dead body reanimated through Vodou magic. The modern concept of zombies as flesh-eating creatures from the cemetery evolved more recently, from the 1968 comedy/horror film, “Night of the Living Dead,” and sequels like “Dawn of the Dead,” “Day of the Dead,” and “Return of the Living Dead.”_
**[Gaines: Journalists conflate all ‘immigrants’ in deportation reporting](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [COMPLETE COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _As an undergrad, I worked many a job, often alongside people who were not born here in the United States. Some were here legally, some not. They comprised a variety of ages, life experiences, dreams, fears, etc.–just as you’d expect with any other group of humans._
**[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|

clean_body: The Battle for Colorado’s Future – GOP Chair Race, 2026 Elections & What’s Next | | | | March 16, 2025 SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST By Heidi Ganahl | | Dear Readers, In today's Sunday News Digest, I am giving you an inside look into my newest episode of Unleashed with Heidi Ganahl! You can , or watch on or . With just under two weeks until the March 29 Colorado GOP Chair election, the battle for the future of the party is heating up. The next Chair will shape the direction of the party for the next two years, and with the 2026 elections already taking form, the stakes couldn’t be higher. GOP Chair Race: The Candidates & Their Plans We now have a clearer picture of how some candidates plan to lead. Rocky Mountain Voice sent out a , and while some still have not responded, Brita Horn, Darcy Schoening, and Kevin McCarney have . positions herself as a unifier, focused on grassroots engagement, bringing factions together, and restoring trust in the party’s fundraising efforts. Darcy Schoening wants to build a winning machine by securing donors early, investing in local races, and aggressively closing primaries. Kevin McCarney is pushing a “Colorado First” approach, rebuilding the Trump coalition, and keeping the GOP primary open to unaffiliated voters. A key issue at play? How we nominate our candidates as a party. First, Some candidates want to overturn it and return to a party-only primary process, while others believe opting out is a losing battle. Second, some candidates want to hold a vote of the State Central Committee to move to a “no ballot primary.” Read the to find out where each stands. Legislative Battles: The Good, The Bad & The Ugly Over 497 bills have been introduced this session, and the Democratic majority continues its aggressive push on everything from gun control to abortion expansion. Here’s a quick (mini) : ✅ The Good: ▪️ Adding Resource (Passed) ▪️ Repeal Recycled Paper Carryout Bag Fee (Lost) ▪️ Malpractice Insurers Gender-Affirming Care Minors (Lost) ▪️ Change Confinement Standards (Lost) ▪️ Open-Source Software in Voting Systems (Lost) ▪️ Constitutional (Lost) ▪️ Noncitizen Voter Registration Cancellation (Lost) ❌ The Bad: ▪️ Banning & Rapid-Fire Devices (Under Consideration) ▪️ Worker Protection (Under Consideration) ▪️ Health-Care Payment System Analysis (Under Consideration) ▪️ Treasurer’s Office (Under Consideration) Colorado’s legislature is a runaway train for progressive policies. If conservatives want to stop the tide, grassroots engagement is key. Thank you Kevin Lundberg for the great breakdown on bills . 2026 Elections: The Field Takes Shape With Governor Jared Polis term-limited, speculation about the Democratic field is in full swing. Senator and Attorney General are eyeing a run, while Secretary of State may shift to the AG race, and Congressman Joe Neguse is rumored to be considering it. On the Republican side, names like Barb Kirkmeyer and Joe Oltmann are being floated, while Jason Mikesell, Scott Bottoms and Mark Baisley have announced - but it’s still wide open. Congressional races in CD3 (Jeff Hurd) and CD8 (Gabe Evans) will also be crucial battlegrounds. Call to Action: Be the Leader in Your Community Now more than ever, we need grassroots action. As I shared on , leadership starts in your neighborhood. Become a precinct leader. Attend school board and city council meetings. Write for Rocky Mountain Voice. This is how we take Colorado back. Let’s build a movement that can win. Stay loud, stay engaged, and stay in the fight. In liberty and truth, Heidi | | | TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK Many conservatives and Second Amendment advocates have raised concerns about the constitutionality of SB25-003, a bill that seeks to make it significantly more difficult for Coloradans to exercise their Second Amendment rights. The sheriff in town may be tasked with the administrative burden of weighing in on your future firearm purchase, if Democrat lawmakers have their say. The DOJ is now reviewing whether federal agencies engaged in politically motivated lawfare in the high-profile case of former Mesa County Clerk Tina Peters. In response to the DOJ’s filing, Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser urged the Denver federal court to reject its interference. | LEGISLATIVE SESSION State Rep. Anthony Hartsook is concerned with the tsunami of bills he sees filtering through the legislature. “Numerous bills expanding government control continue to flood the General Assembly, threatening businesses and economic freedom,” he wrote in a report to his District 44 constituency. name: Amanda Chase
subject: Want to be able to vote for Amanda Chase for Governor?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase 2025
body:
Here's how to sign her petition
| | |
---
| |
---

---
| |
---

---
|

---
| Let's Get Amanda Chase's name on the ballot for Virginia Governor
---
|
# ONLY 2 FULL WEEKS LEFT
# UNTIL THE DEADLINE
---
|
# SIGN AMANDA'S PETITION UPDATE
---
|

---
| | [CLICK HERE AND PRINT OFF THE PETITON ](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TyuaOX3we8BUDZM4KKBkatcYmAxOAlWYXpFXhk7Cx_gvFTnfkErEtrJcYv9or8mhKLHWO7z9kG85I5Oc99JIJyMFDr5BH3xex4eBcvYfOj8p507SFlX_dzSPH4zYuNVlo_ryOmAyxOzhlAF7hbCQRVW0Hr_Wkepq&c=b07iwy5qfz9dggjEDWubjtQKbUvJgJljbKGrzWInTHYomIq7MYY1wA==&ch=KjIoa4UhPQD0L0CD9ES9o9HH5puIxbtmPQ_iHL4TLLK2ePVtswH0_A==)
---
|
Dear Hello I'm so impressed with many of you. We already have so many signatures in hand, we haven't finished counting them.
That said, we still have a ways to go.
Many of you are still collecting. Please go ahead and Fed Ex to us the ones that you have already signed in front of a notary, so our team can verify the signatures. Then, please continue collecting. Remember, we need 400 signatures in each Congressional District.
I am so impressed with the commitment level of so many of you. And I want you to know I am just as committed, working every hour of the day to collect and verify our signatures at night.
One volunteer in the 9th Congressional District said he is using his week of vacation next week to collect signatures to get my name on the ballot. He hits the doors every day after he gets home from work and was a huge door knocker for President Trump.
Another volunteer in the 1st Congressional District just had heart surgery and was back out at a popular hunting goods store collecting signatures.
Another team of volunteers in the 7th Congressional District organized a table outside of a popular store with the permission of the owner and collected signatures from 11am to 8pm on Saturday.
The stories of people excited about my run for Governor have been so incredibly inspiring of passionate volunteers who want a governor who will get things done like President Trump has done at the national level.
Personally, I'm committed too. This Saturday morning morning we left Appomattox to head to the Maple Festival to collect signatures there. Then a pro-2A event in Culpeper where we collected signatures with a team of volunteers there. If it's daylight, you will find me in a parking lot or pro-2A event collecting signatures somewhere in our great state.
That said, we have a ways to go. Once it's dark, we are verifying signatures to make sure they are valid. Once we finish processing all of the signatures in hand, we will start reporting to you numbers of where we are. Right now there are too many to count.
Keep this in mind. We need a minimum of 400 signatures in each Congressional District. That's about 35 pages of signatures. So please keep going to parking lots across Virginia to find gracious people willing to sign our petition.
Remember, in order for my name to appear on the ballot, we have to collect the necessary signatures to get my name on the ballot.
We must collect 10,000 valid signatures, 400 in each Congressional District, and as a campaign, turn the signatures into the State Board of Elections before Thursday, April 3 at 5pm.
We would like to receive all forms before Monday, March 31, so that we can properly organize and vet the signatures before turning them in to the Virginia State Board of Elections.
Hundreds of you have reached out and are actively collecting signatures. Thank you. It's only through your efforts that my name will appear on the ballot. Currently, our campaign is 100 percent run by volunteers in each locality. We have no paid staff. The amazing organization you've seen from my campaign is all run by volunteers who want an American First Governor, who can beat Abigail Spanberger in November.
If 500 people collect just 2 pages or 24 signatures, we meet our goal. Will you commit to helping?
Thanks to many of you who have already answered that call. If everyone does their part, you will be able to vote for me in June. If not, you won't have a choice.
We have two full weeks to go until our deadline, and we've made tremendous progress.
We have a team of grassroots volunteers in every Congressional District across the state actively collecting signatures. To join a team in your area, please reply to this email and I will personally connect you with volunteers in your area.
Remember. I said yes, but only if you help me.
In order for you to vote for me on June 17, please print off the attached petition and collect the signatures of your family, friends and neighbors, so that I can get on the ballot.
WE CANNOT COLLECT SIGNATURES ONLINE.
Signatures must be witnessed in person by signing an old fashioned paper petition ballot.
Are you ready to say yes? Here's what you need to do:

1. Print off the attached form on one sheet of paper, front and back.
2. Witness your friends, family and neighbors sign the petition form. Please make sure they actually sign the form. Please also make sure their writing is legible so that signatures can be verified.
3. Keep in mind that you can't sign your own petition form.
4. Please collect signatures by[ Congressional District.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TyuaOX3we8BUDZM4KKBkatcYmAxOAlWYXpFXhk7Cx_gvFTnfkErEtoMpXkKVhg7gwyfdOf_5vNg7ObM-UEv123RfJfTDeFwEAROn2Y40z4DUpOo88XwJ128wR3Km5tl2HBHeGlTtrlYLfvQXoFqr95mXVjJTJvsaX8skBome4z35taQUXvLUzdJR6W7nA5WE7a7VksTfut0=&c=b07iwy5qfz9dggjEDWubjtQKbUvJgJljbKGrzWInTHYomIq7MYY1wA==&ch=KjIoa4UhPQD0L0CD9ES9o9HH5puIxbtmPQ_iHL4TLLK2ePVtswH0_A==) One Congressional District per form. You can write the Congressional District in the upper right hand corner.
5. Get the petition notarized at your local bank for free.
6. Drop the petition off in person to: 2023 Church Street, Appomattox, VA 24522
7. Or Fed Ex the petition to:

Friends of Amanda Chase 2023 Church Street #250 Appomattox, VA 24522
For security reasons we will not publish a public calendar, but you can call us at 804 370 8727 or email me at amanda@chaseforva.com to find out when we will be in your area and to coordinate picking up your petitions with the volunteer coordinator in your area.
---
| Please use the link below to print off the petition forms.
Please remember the forms must be double sided and printed front and back. You must also witness every signature and visit your local bank to have a notary sign off on your petition form before mailing.
Please note that we need the original copy. Photocopies are not allowed.
If you have any questions please email Craig at craig@chaseforva.com or call 804 370 8727. If you have trouble printing the forms, we can mail you the forms.
I can do this with your help. Please print off a petition now. Don't wait. Get your immediate family to sign the petition and then get your friends at church and work to sign your petition. Time is not on our side. Less than 19 days to collect signatures.
If not you, then who?
Please click the button below to print your petitions today and start collecting signatures as you run your errands throughout your day.
---
| | [CHASE PETITION FORMS CLICK HERE](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TyuaOX3we8BUDZM4KKBkatcYmAxOAlWYXpFXhk7Cx_gvFTnfkErEtgAq6dxM4I1GHx5OMGqFuPL0RgtlR0-FjzbewWlpKLdhBN-2XZCcpnjjJ3Ga7SsHylth13O4FHszK7sENpC10R2mxP_xUQqajvKDNbw3bNYw&c=b07iwy5qfz9dggjEDWubjtQKbUvJgJljbKGrzWInTHYomIq7MYY1wA==&ch=KjIoa4UhPQD0L0CD9ES9o9HH5puIxbtmPQ_iHL4TLLK2ePVtswH0_A==)
---
| | [CLICK HERE TO FIND YOUR CONGRESSIONAL DISTRICT](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TyuaOX3we8BUDZM4KKBkatcYmAxOAlWYXpFXhk7Cx_gvFTnfkErEtklismGSlyCwq5LUdalihXnHBtTpliYuJpu-c3roytKj8_Rbptz4_1tRfu3z--k3vJpfcLN4mVHUphWRnvKIKqlKr9l94MAotR2Nj7Hhe-DZ5zbV6xDyvCIpv0CyrLnWEMYhnYFhP9B7IUDDTrU1np8=&c=b07iwy5qfz9dggjEDWubjtQKbUvJgJljbKGrzWInTHYomIq7MYY1wA==&ch=KjIoa4UhPQD0L0CD9ES9o9HH5puIxbtmPQ_iHL4TLLK2ePVtswH0_A==)

# MAP OF CONGRESSIONAL DISTRICTS
---
| Adopt a Congressional District. We need 400 signatures in each of the 11 Congressional Districts. Please text or call 804 370 8727 and let us know where you are collecting signatures so I know where I need to focus our efforts.
Please be sure to collect signatures by Congressional District. If you aren't sure which Congressional District you live in, you can check the map below.

# IMPORTANT DATES
---
|
Petition Signatures due to State Board of Elections: Thursday, April 3, 2025, at 5pm (please get your forms to us ASAP)
Primary Date: Tuesday, June 17, 2025 General Election Date: Tuesday, November 4, 2025

---
|
# PLEASE GIVE GENEROUSLY
---
| Abigail Spanberger has raised over $15 million dollars for her campaign for governor, but the people are not with her. She's out of touch with most Virginians. And I'm not intimidated by her. Once people realize where she gets her money her campaign is going to implode.
Please give your best donation to help pay for gas to travel the state. Thankfully, many across the state already know me. So we don't have to spend as much as our opponents who have no statewide name ID.
Our campaign is run by volunteers not paid campaign staff and operatives. But we do have to pay event fees for gun shows, entrance to events and office supplies. Any contribution you can make is greatly appreciated.

clean_body: Here's how to sign her petition | | | | | | | | | Let's Get Amanda Chase's name on the ballot for Virginia Governor | ONLY 2 FULL WEEKS LEFT UNTIL THE DEADLINE | SIGN AMANDA'S PETITION UPDATE | | | == | Dear Hello I'm so impressed with many of you. We already have so many signatures in hand, we haven't finished counting them. That said, we still have a ways to go. Many of you are still collecting. Please go ahead and Fed Ex to us the ones that you have already signed in front of a notary, so our team can verify the signatures. Then, please continue collecting. Remember, we need 400 signatures in each Congressional District. I am so impressed with the commitment level of so many of you. And I want you to know I am just as committed, working every hour of the day to collect and verify our signatures at night. One volunteer in the 9th Congressional District said he is using his week of vacation next week to collect signatures to get my name on the ballot. He hits the doors every day after he gets home from work and was a huge door knocker for President Trump. Another volunteer in the 1st Congressional District just had heart surgery and was back out at a popular hunting goods store collecting signatures. Another team of volunteers in the 7th Congressional District organized a table outside of a popular store with the permission of the owner and collected signatures from 11am to 8pm on Saturday. The stories of people excited about my run for Governor have been so incredibly inspiring of passionate volunteers who want a governor who will get things done like President Trump has done at the national level. Personally, I'm committed too. This Saturday morning morning we left Appomattox to head to the Maple Festival to collect signatures there. Then a pro-2A event in Culpeper where we collected signatures with a team of volunteers there. If it's daylight, you will find me in a parking lot or pro-2A event collecting signatures somewhere in our great state. That said, we have a ways to go. Once it's dark, we are verifying signatures to make sure they are valid. Once we finish processing all of the signatures in hand, we will start reporting to you numbers of where we are. Right now there are too many to count. Keep this in mind. We need a minimum of 400 signatures in each Congressional District. That's about 35 pages of signatures. So please keep going to parking lots across Virginia to find gracious people willing to sign our petition. Remember, in order for my name to appear on the ballot, we have to collect the necessary signatures to get my name on the ballot. We must collect 10,000 valid signatures, 400 in each Congressional District, and as a campaign, turn the signatures into the State Board of Elections before Thursday, April 3 at 5pm. We would like to receive all forms before Monday, March 31, so that we can properly organize and vet the signatures before turning them in to the Virginia State Board of Elections. Hundreds of you have reached out and are actively collecting signatures. Thank you. It's only through your efforts that my name will appear on the ballot. Currently, our campaign is 100 percent run by volunteers in each locality. We have no paid staff. The amazing organization you've seen from my campaign is all run by volunteers who want an American First Governor, who can beat Abigail Spanberger in November. If 500 people collect just 2 pages or 24 signatures, we meet our goal. Will you commit to helping? Thanks to many of you who have already answered that call. If everyone does their part, you will be able to vote for me in June. If not, you won't have a choice. We have two full weeks to go until our deadline, and we've made tremendous progress. We have a team of grassroots volunteers in every Congressional District across the state actively collecting signatures. To join a team in your area, please reply to this email and I will personally connect you with volunteers in your area. Remember. I said yes, but only if you help me. In order for you to vote for me on June 17, please print off the attached petition and collect the signatures of your family, friends and neighbors, so that I can get on the ballot. WE CANNOT COLLECT SIGNATURES ONLINE. Signatures must be witnessed in person by signing an old fashioned paper petition ballot. Are you ready to say yes? Here's what you need to do: 1. Print off the attached form on one sheet of paper, front and back. 2. Witness your friends, family and neighbors sign the petition form. Please make sure they actually sign the form. Please also make sure their writing is legible so that signatures can be verified. 3. Keep in mind that you can't sign your own petition form. 4. Please collect signatures by== One Congressional District per form. You can write the Congressional District in the upper right hand corner. 5. Get the petition notarized at your local bank for free. 6. Drop the petition off in person to: 2023 Church Street, Appomattox, VA 24522 7. Or Fed Ex the petition to: Friends of Amanda Chase 2023 Church Street #250 Appomattox, VA 24522 For security reasons we will not publish a public calendar, but you can call us at 804 370 8727 or email me at amanda@chaseforva.com to find out when we will be in your area and to coordinate picking up your petitions with the volunteer coordinator in your area. | Please use the link below to print off the petition forms. Please remember the forms must be double sided and printed front and back. You must also witness every signature and visit your local bank to have a notary sign off on your petition form before mailing. Please note that we need the original copy. Photocopies are not allowed. If you have any questions please email Craig at craig@chaseforva.com or call 804 370 8727. If you have trouble printing the forms, we can mail you the forms. I can do this with your help. Please print off a petition now. Don't wait. Get your immediate family to sign the petition and then get your friends at church and work to sign your petition. Time is not on our side. Less than 19 days to collect signatures. If not you, then who? Please click the button below to print your petitions today and start collecting signatures as you run your errands throughout your day. | | == | | == | | | | | | | | | MAP OF CONGRESSIONAL DISTRICTS | Adopt a Congressional District. We need 400 signatures in each of the 11 Congressional Districts. Please text or call 804 370 8727 and let us know where you are collecting signatures so I know where I need to focus our efforts. Please be sure to collect signatures by Congressional District. If you aren't sure which Congressional District you live in, you can check the map below. | | | IMPORTANT DATES | Petition Signatures due to State Board of Elections: Thursday, April 3, 2025, at 5pm (please get your forms to us ASAP) Primary Date: Tuesday, June 17, 2025 General Election Date: Tuesday, November 4, 2025 | PLEASE GIVE GENEROUSLY | Abigail Spanberger has raised over $15 million dollars for her campaign for governor, but the people are not with her. She's out of touch with most Virginians. And I'm not intimidated by her. Once people realize where she gets her money her campaign is going to implode. Please give your best donation to help pay for gas to travel the state. Thankfully, many across the state already know me. So we don't have to spend as much as our opponents who have no statewide name ID. Our campaign is run by volunteers not paid campaign staff and operatives. But we do have to pay event fees for gun shows, entrance to events and office supplies. Any contribution you can make is greatly appreciated. | | == | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase 2025 | | | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Box 250 | Appomattox, VA 24522 US | | | | | | |
