---|---
| [](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=c27413b9ed&e=77eef70c61)
---
| | Friend,

Recently, King County's far-left leadership proposed a series of devastating cuts to law enforcement and criminal justice resources. In total, the proposals amount to nearly $70 million in cuts to core law enforcement and criminal justice resources that would**slash the number of unincorporated Sheriff 's Deputies in half and could lead to the firing of 90 criminal prosecutors!**

I have one question--does this seem right to you?

It sure doesn't seem right to me. That's why I recently sent a letter to county leadership urging them to, in addition to removing these cuts altogether, IMMEDIATELY restore $15 million in funding to our most essential criminal justice responsibilities by zeroing out funding for soft-on-crime approaches that are failing our communities.
[Listen to my interview with Jason Rantz about this issue.](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=4f2c06bf7e&e=77eef70c61)
Just when you thought anti-police extremism in downtown Seattle couldn't get any more out of control, these proposals directly cut our law enforcement capabilities at a time when they are needed more than ever. \--------------------
**STAND AGAINST THESE DEVASTATING CUTS TO LAW ENFORCEMENT BY SUPPORTING MY CAMPAIGN!**
[**DONATE NOW**](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=5b59f0c073&e=77eef70c61)
\-------------------- But here's what really makes me upset--King County's far-left leadership is proposing these devastating cuts to law enforcement while the County is attempting to pass **_five new taxes_** in 2025. That's right, they want to **_INCREASE_** your taxes dramatically while **_DECREASING_** the number of Sheriff's Deputies on our streets.

Friend, stopping these devastating cuts to law enforcement that would slash the number of unincorporated Sheriff's Deputies in half is one of the main reasons our campaign is so important. I am the only member of the King County Council forcefully speaking out against these cuts, and I need your help! **_Would you be willing to_**[**make a small donation of $50, $35, or even $25 right now**](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=0fc603c5ba&e=77eef70c61)** _to make sure our message is heard?_** \-------------------- [**DONATE HERE**](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=908a944d05&e=77eef70c61) \-------------------- Your support empowers me to raise awareness and stop these devastating cuts. Thank you!

Reagan Dunn

subject: Let’s Celebrate a Brighter Future!
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gay Valimont
body:
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/70756F655332783372636F454353396562687376694F485948735343712B763672586D4157707A676459726C636F6733786F6E2B526572766D6752546567474F/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-01-10%20at%208.23.20%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Today, on St. Patrick’s Day, I’m reminded of the spirit of resilience, unity, and the rich heritage that brings us together. Whether you’re Irish by blood or simply by heart, this holiday is a time to celebrate community, hope, and the belief that every person deserves a fair chance to thrive.
In many ways, our campaign reflects that same spirit, and we need your help within the last 14 days to make a difference. Just as St. Patrick’s Day reminds us to embrace our traditions and work together, I am committed to forging a future that honors every Floridian’s hard work and dreams. From fighting for better public schools to ensuring our seniors have secure access to healthcare, I believe in policies that uplift everyone—just as our shared traditions uplift our community.
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/49413568474E5A794E4F7365707678434A486E36426A362B48377A6C517770597A4D616B67627446526A6C77663145333875507A75536A58767A5A7377566768/editor_images/patt.gif]
Let’s celebrate with a renewed sense of purpose and unity. I invite you to join me in not only enjoying the festivities but also in committing to a brighter future for Florida’s 1st District. Together, we can build a community as vibrant and welcoming as the spirit of this special day.
Donate now before it is too late! With a bit of St. Patty’s luck and your contribution of $10, we can gift our communities better policy and leadership! 🍀🍀. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
Express Donate: $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=express_lane_5]
Express Donate: $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=express_lane_10]
Express Donate: $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=express_lane_25]
Express Donate: $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=express_lane_100]
Or, donate another amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other]
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/49413568474E5A794E4F7365707678434A486E36426A362B48377A6C517770597A4D616B67627446526A6C77663145333875507A75536A58767A5A7377566768/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-01-25%20at%209.53.29%E2%80%AFAM.png]
May your day be filled with good luck, great company, and the promise of positive change. Join us to make these wishes a long-term reality for the U.S.! Happy St. Patrick’s Day!
Sláinte,
Gay Valimont
Democratic Candidate for Congress
Florida’s 1st District
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP59/1/107746/images/GaySignoff.png]

Gulf Breeze, FL 32562
United States

# [Minnesota Supreme Court Briefing: #transparencyproject - Part 15.](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2066440&post_id=158404542&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU4NDA0NTQyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDIyNDE4MjcsImV4cCI6MTc0NDgzMzgyNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIwNjY0NDAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ciFsUeY6B7XO0XvEI0SLWxv2zId3ATxYYfDUQVx8mLs)
### Oral Arguments are expected this spring!
| | [Benda For Commonsense](https://substack.com/@bendasense)
---
| Mar 17
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bendasense)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2066440&post_id=158404542&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU4NDA0NTQyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0MjI0MTgyNywiZXhwIjoxNzQ0ODMzODI3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjA2NjQ0MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.eYngrS_T_e76OcwTpV6QRDK1TqdhwAagA4OyIYpuhjs&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2066440&post_id=158404542&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU4NDA0NTQyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDIyNDE4MjcsImV4cCI6MTc0NDgzMzgyNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIwNjY0NDAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ciFsUeY6B7XO0XvEI0SLWxv2zId3ATxYYfDUQVx8mLs&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2066440&post_id=158404542&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU4NDA0NTQyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDIyNDE4MjcsImV4cCI6MTc0NDgzMzgyNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIwNjY0NDAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ciFsUeY6B7XO0XvEI0SLWxv2zId3ATxYYfDUQVx8mLs)

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bendasense/p/minnesota-supreme-court-briefing?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

On December 17, 2024 the Minnesota Supreme Court lifted the "stay of the proceedings" and our case has now completed the briefing stage. This means that all written arguments have been presented to the Supreme Court and the case of _Benda for Common-sense v. Anderson, 999 N.W.2d 893_(Minn. App. 2023) will now be considered for review. In-person oral arguments at the Minnesota Supreme Court will be scheduled soon!!
| Benda For Commonsense v. Anderson, 999 N...114KB ∙ PDF file
---|---
| [Download](https://substack.com/redirect/207773b3-776f-4889-b37b-f4d9678ed098?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
@Bendasense Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5kYXNlbnNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.eh8Ru8_90RhCbS6Y69Nu2g4G2iwhuhuIH1Dblv_gUb8?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=158404542)
The parties that brought the lawsuit were my non-profit corporation, Benda for Commonsense and Kathleen Hagen. Hagen is an Engineering Project Manager by trade, specializing in Mechanical and Systems Engineering. Professionally, Hagen spends a significant amount of her time making sure processes are efficient, safe, and secure for a fortune 500 company. Hagen was also employed as a head election judge in Rice County until she was removed following the 2020 election. We are now known as the "Appellants."
This matter started in Rice County district court on August 23, 2022, when the Appellants initiated a three part/count lawsuit against the Director of Rice County Property Tax and Elections. Counts I and II were requests that the district court compel compliance with document disclosure requests under the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act. Count III requested that the district court require the correction of an error or omission relating to the use of modem hardware embedded in the election machines utilized by Rice County. Minnesota has a specific statute that allows for the courts to remedy errors or mistakes in the administration of elections. [Minn. Stat. 204B.44.](https://substack.com/redirect/81e92a87-0bca-4cdc-86b8-6b6c8bdd864a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
On January 17, 2023 the Rice County district court dismissed all three counts of our lawsuit. On February 23, 2023, we filed our appeal with the Minnesota Court of Appeals to review whether the district court properly ruled on these matters. A summary of this portion of the lawsuit can be [found here](https://substack.com/redirect/804fa4b4-c498-406d-9d4e-423accb85823?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). On December 18, 2023, the Minnesota Court of Appeals took a [path of least resistance](https://substack.com/redirect/25db8a55-9112-4d9f-958a-b2b43e15db19?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and affirmed the district court's dismissal of all of our claims.
The significant determination in this order is as follows:
"... to establish the district court's subject-matter jurisdiction over the action, appellants were required to serve all candidates running for offices in that election with a copy of the petition."
A quick review of the canvassing results from Rice County from the 2022 election show that there were 65 offices on the ballot, with multiple candidates for each race.
| Rice County 202 Canvassing Results39.3MB ∙ PDF file
---|---
| [Download](https://substack.com/redirect/226ab29d-b9e2-4034-889a-2a2d33b28890?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
You read that correctly -- under the current ruling by the Minnesota Court of Appeals, we would have been required to serve over 130 candidates to even get our court action under review by the district court. The randomness of this ruling has a legal term: "drive-by jurisdictional ruling" \- meaning it is not applied consistently and elevates a procedural requirement into a jurisdictional bar to entry.
Fortunately, on February 28, 2024, the Minnesota Supreme Court granted our Petition for discretionary review of the dismissal of our lawsuit.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/8ae29099-9478-4212-ae42-bf8c31bc34c4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |
#### [We are going to the Minnesota Supreme Court!! #transparencyproject Part 13:](https://substack.com/redirect/8ae29099-9478-4212-ae42-bf8c31bc34c4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Benda For Commonsense](https://substack.com/profile/107252626-benda-for-commonsense)| | *
---|---|---
| May 29, 2024
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/8ae29099-9478-4212-ae42-bf8c31bc34c4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In a bit of an unusual twist, our case was put on hold pending the outcome of another case under review by the Minnesota Supreme Court. Given that the Court decision in that case would impact the issue of subject-matter jurisdiction in our case, we had to wait until that case was decided, which did not occur until October 23, 2024.
With that background, the following primary issue is now under review by the Minnesota Supreme Court:
"Whether the Appellants were required under Minn. Stat. § 204B.44(a)(4) to serve all candidates running for office at the commencement of the action to acquire subject-matter jurisdiction."
We argue to the Court that it would be inconsistent with the intent of the statute to require that all candidates up and down the ballot be served with every petition as a pre-requisite to the court even obtaining jurisdiction of the matter. As we articulate in our written submissions/briefs, this would also be inconsistent with how Minnesota courts have previously handled these cases.
These are excerpts from our primary brief:
#### "**Every voter 's vote is entitled to be counted once. It must be correctly counted and reported**." _Gray v. Sanders_ , 372 U.S. 368, 380 (1963).
> It is not a matter of if, but when, judicial intervention will be required to remedy some type of hiccup in the electronic counting and reporting of votes in Minnesota. The catch-all provision of Minnesota's Errors and Omissions statute is clearly drafted to allow judicial intervention regarding "any duty concerning an election". Minn. Stat. §204B.44(a)(4).
> * * *
> The current appeal relates to one of the more onerous threats to the electronic voting system in Minnesota, wireless/modem connectivity to transmit early election results to the Minnesota Secretary of State
The brief continues,
> Rather than acknowledge this heightened risk and embrace the need for potential judicial scrutiny, the Respondents/election officials that are parties in this matter advocate for a restrictive and limited jurisdictional scope of Minnesota's dynamic election review process. Respondents' defensive and short-sighted request that this Court impose multiple subject-matter jurisdictional deterrents should be rejected. These inapplicable jurisdictional barriers include an improperly strict application of a claim-processing service requirement; and an interpretation of the Errors and Omissions statute that strips away the original jurisdiction of district courts in Minnesota.
> As set forth below, a careful review of Minn. Stat. § 204B.44 establishes that the legislature did not intend to rank these provisions as having the jurisdictional consequences advocated by Respondents. Instead, as articulated by the United States Supreme Court, the Respondents' requests to avoid scrutiny are simply a request for, "drive-by jurisdictional rulings" that "should be accorded no precedential effect" on the question of subject matter jurisdiction. _Arbaugh v. Y & H Corp.,_ 546 U.S. 500, 522 (2006).
As articulated throughout our briefing, the Minnesota legislature clearly did not "rank" the service requirement found in Minn. Stat. § 204B.44(b) as a requirement of obtaining subject matter jurisdiction. Instead, this statutory language was clearly added to, "create an efficiency in the Errors and Omissions Petitions in election matters." This was supported by comprehensive legislative history that the language relied upon in dimissing our claims was added at the specific request of the judiciary branch to "create an efficiency in the Errors and Omissions Petitions in election matters."
In our Reply Brief, we request that the lower court ruling be reversed and the matter be remanded/sent back to Rice County district court,
> "As elections become more complicated, the judiciary should refrain from limiting its scope of review and ability to address unforeseen circumstances. In fact-intensive circumstances, this review may be best conducted in a district court forum. As shown in this case, this often requires the building of a detailed record, motion practice, and appellate review."
We are requesting that the Minnesota Supreme Court reverse the Minnesota Court of Appeals' drive-by jurisdictional ruling, and send this case back to district court to proceed through discovery, and a full trial to determine the legality of the use of modems in the election process.
Stay tuned for more details!!

clean_body: Oral Arguments are expected this spring! | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Oral Arguments are expected this spring! | | | Mar 17 | | | | | | | | | | | | On December 17, 2024 the Minnesota Supreme Court lifted the "stay of the proceedings" and our case has now completed the briefing stage. This means that all written arguments have been presented to the Supreme Court and the case of Benda for Common-sense v. Anderson, 999 N.W.2d 893(Minn. App. 2023) will now be considered for review. In-person oral arguments at the Minnesota Supreme Court will be scheduled soon!! | Benda For Commonsense v. Anderson, 999 N...114KB ∙ PDF file ---|--- | @Bendasense Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. The parties that brought the lawsuit were my non-profit corporation, Benda for Commonsense and Kathleen Hagen. Hagen is an Engineering Project Manager by trade, specializing in Mechanical and Systems Engineering. Professionally, Hagen spends a significant amount of her time making sure processes are efficient, safe, and secure for a fortune 500 company. Hagen was also employed as a head election judge in Rice County until she was removed following the 2020 election. We are now known as the "Appellants." This matter started in Rice County district court on August 23, 2022, when the Appellants initiated a three part/count lawsuit against the Director of Rice County Property Tax and Elections. Counts I and II were requests that the district court compel compliance with document disclosure requests under the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act. Count III requested that the district court require the correction of an error or omission relating to the use of modem hardware embedded in the election machines utilized by Rice County. Minnesota has a specific statute that allows for the courts to remedy errors or mistakes in the administration of elections. On January 17, 2023 the Rice County district court dismissed all three counts of our lawsuit. On February 23, 2023, we filed our appeal with the Minnesota Court of Appeals to review whether the district court properly ruled on these matters. A summary of this portion of the lawsuit can be . On December 18, 2023, the Minnesota Court of Appeals took a and affirmed the district court's dismissal of all of our claims. The significant determination in this order is as follows: "... to establish the district court's subject-matter jurisdiction over the action, appellants were required to serve all candidates running for offices in that election with a copy of the petition." A quick review of the canvassing results from Rice County from the 2022 election show that there were 65 offices on the ballot, with multiple candidates for each race. | Rice County 202 Canvassing Results39.3MB ∙ PDF file ---|--- | You read that correctly -- under the current ruling by the Minnesota Court of Appeals, we would have been required to serve over 130 candidates to even get our court action under review by the district court. The randomness of this ruling has a legal term: "drive-by jurisdictional ruling" \- meaning it is not applied consistently and elevates a procedural requirement into a jurisdictional bar to entry. Fortunately, on February 28, 2024, the Minnesota Supreme Court granted our Petition for discretionary review of the dismissal of our lawsuit. | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | May 29, 2024 | In a bit of an unusual twist, our case was put on hold pending the outcome of another case under review by the Minnesota Supreme Court. Given that the Court decision in that case would impact the issue of subject-matter jurisdiction in our case, we had to wait until that case was decided, which did not occur until October 23, 2024. With that background, the following primary issue is now under review by the Minnesota Supreme Court: "Whether the Appellants were required under Minn. Stat. § 204B.44(a)(4) to serve all candidates running for office at the commencement of the action to acquire subject-matter jurisdiction." We argue to the Court that it would be inconsistent with the intent of the statute to require that all candidates up and down the ballot be served with every petition as a pre-requisite to the court even obtaining jurisdiction of the matter. As we articulate in our written submissions/briefs, this would also be inconsistent with how Minnesota courts have previously handled these cases. These are excerpts from our primary brief: "Every voter 's vote is entitled to be counted once. It must be correctly counted and reported." Gray v. Sanders , 372 U.S. 368, 380 (1963). It is not a matter of if, but when, judicial intervention will be required to remedy some type of hiccup in the electronic counting and reporting of votes in Minnesota. The catch-all provision of Minnesota's Errors and Omissions statute is clearly drafted to allow judicial intervention regarding "any duty concerning an election". Minn. Stat. §204B.44(a)(4). The current appeal relates to one of the more onerous threats to the electronic voting system in Minnesota, wireless/modem connectivity to transmit early election results to the Minnesota Secretary of State The brief continues, Rather than acknowledge this heightened risk and embrace the need for potential judicial scrutiny, the Respondents/election officials that are parties in this matter advocate for a restrictive and limited jurisdictional scope of Minnesota's dynamic election review process. Respondents' defensive and short-sighted request that this Court impose multiple subject-matter jurisdictional deterrents should be rejected. These inapplicable jurisdictional barriers include an improperly strict application of a claim-processing service requirement; and an interpretation of the Errors and Omissions statute that strips away the original jurisdiction of district courts in Minnesota. As set forth below, a careful review of Minn. Stat. § 204B.44 establishes that the legislature did not intend to rank these provisions as having the jurisdictional consequences advocated by Respondents. Instead, as articulated by the United States Supreme Court, the Respondents' requests to avoid scrutiny are simply a request for, "drive-by jurisdictional rulings" that "should be accorded no precedential effect" on the question of subject matter jurisdiction. Arbaugh v. Y & H Corp., 546 U.S. 500, 522 (2006). As articulated throughout our briefing, the Minnesota legislature clearly did not "rank" the service requirement found in Minn. Stat. § 204B.44(b) as a requirement of obtaining subject matter jurisdiction. Instead, this statutory language was clearly added to, "create an efficiency in the Errors and Omissions Petitions in election matters." This was supported by comprehensive legislative history that the language relied upon in dimissing our claims was added at the specific request of the judiciary branch to "create an efficiency in the Errors and Omissions Petitions in election matters." In our Reply Brief, we request that the lower court ruling be reversed and the matter be remanded/sent back to Rice County district court, "As elections become more complicated, the judiciary should refrain from limiting its scope of review and ability to address unforeseen circumstances. In fact-intensive circumstances, this review may be best conducted in a district court forum. As shown in this case, this often requires the building of a detailed record, motion practice, and appellate review." We are requesting that the Minnesota Supreme Court reverse the Minnesota Court of Appeals' drive-by jurisdictional ruling, and send this case back to district court to proceed through discovery, and a full trial to determine the legality of the use of modems in the election process. Stay tuned for more details!! * @Bendasense Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Benda For Commonsense 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

# Calling All Wisconsin Volunteers!
---
| Hi, Friend!
---
Do you live in Wisconsin and want to get involved in your upcoming elections? Join us!
We are rallying Wisconsinites “on the ground” who would like to participate in in-person Election Challenger training this Saturday, March 22nd. If you are a registered voter in WI, please reach out to [wisconsin@truethevote.org](mailto:wisconsin@truethevote.org) so we can get you signed up!
If you are already planning on serving in your election and don’t necessarily require training, you can also download our [WI Quick Reference Guide ](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.bYZLwP2tKNW52PF5H5Fs6Uv_zI6ORG-2dl91JLqNhJNRmno19XjJNGTkt5hzpvq4vbg4AMTD6NyC7HQpLs_tuDx2Y984XWZXD3sjt8g04pHe7pSHcj--M51uhLOQt1XW_YXFLv-ifg15FMF6jBCGq7BBrlZMt4sMV5XLEwJtosZd1ZHSUSznjh45YP27cq3puoRTZDTFQa_YPmmiMD7gp_I1k08b-E-wPB6ggY_x-R8u5Z9WR4o7OOzcOjNqMTICvIBwNq0XOTH2OmLFP3BrB7mjg-xpawyt-6DPYqR_uaASokhDR2aJWZQC5HFFb18xBpkx8m9Doz1b4U3tFoFC9gDfYYkSr-4f59h_PQq28n3nh2LPuwZVSaULRgqy-A38/4ev/9o3ZEo-RTNWItQqZRmTQyA/h1/h001.qZmHSnywgil5mXZo2hxUUEOBcgapmS_UCzZRknCv81s)to take with you to the polls.

clean_body: | | | March 17, 2025 | | Calling All Wisconsin Volunteers! | Hi, Friend! Do you live in Wisconsin and want to get involved in your upcoming elections? Join us! We are rallying Wisconsinites “on the ground” who would like to participate in in-person Election Challenger training this Saturday, March 22nd. If you are a registered voter in WI, please reach out to mailto:wisconsin@truethevote.org so we can get you signed up! If you are already planning on serving in your election and don’t necessarily require training, you can also download our take with you to the polls. | For all volunteers in other states, be sure to to stay informed - we have more trainings coming for everyone! IV3, LegiTrack, TTV evergreen and brand new initiatives are coming soon and we will be providing trainings for all of the above. Please consider contributing to our multiple initiatives so we can continue the pertinent mission of securing our elections. Your tax-deductible contribution fuels our ongoing projects. | ) Here at True the Vote, we cannot express our gratitude for each and every one of you who choose to dedicate your time towards continuing to secure our elections. God has paved a way for us to finally have the ability to fix what is broken, and your involvement is crucial and appreciated! | | Share True the Vote You currently have 0 referrals. | | Or copy and paste this link to others: | If you have any questions, our team is always happy to assist in any way we can. name: Adam Schiff
subject: “Vladimir Putin’s personal wishlist”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/62?t=1001&akid=8369%2E5257348%2EjFsdJH ]Adam Schiff for Senate

Derek, we need
to talk about Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.

Because the first few weeks of Trump’s second presidency have proven what
we knew to be true:

Trump can be bought and influenced by men with power and wealth. Flattery
itself goes a long way with Trump, and dictators are taking full advantage
of his greed and egotism.

Last time he was in the White House, Trump shamelessly cozied up to
Vladimir Putin and the Kremlin. He literally exchanged love letters with
the dictator of North Korea and has a warmer relationship with Kim Jong-un
than the Canadian Prime Minister. He even threatened to abandon our NATO
allies if there was an attack in Europe.

This time around, as our allies in Ukraine fight back against Russia,
Trump is once again siding with Putin.

He withdrew military aid from Ukraine and cut off intelligence sharing. He
publicly tried to belittle Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy — the
same president he tried to extort in his first term by withholding
military support in exchange for an investigation of Biden, which led to
his first impeachment. He insists that Vladimir Putin is committed to
peace — even though Putin launched this war, and is a bloodthirsty
dictator.

As Axios put it, these are “moves that could have been plucked from
Vladimir Putin’s personal wishlist.”

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q12025_abs_em.9NB2Ka&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250317_PM1_V1_S1&t=1004&akid=8369%2E5257348%2EjFsdJH ]Axios: Trump's stunning string of Putin-friendly moves President
Trump's decision to suspend all military aid to Ukraine is the latest in a
string of moves that could have been plucked from Vladimir Putin's
personal wishlist.

Derek, Donald
Trump’s abandonment of Ukraine is a dangerous signal to countries
worldwide. Democratic countries are terrified of what’s to come as Trump
abandons our allies in favor of oligarchs.

I have stood up against authoritarianism and in support of democracy for
my entire career. From my chairmanship of the House Intelligence Committee
to leading Trump’s impeachment, to serving on the January 6 Select
Committee, I have always put my oath of office and the Constitution first.
Always.

I’m continuing this vital work to protect our democracy from the U.S.
Senate. [ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/2907?c0=q12025_abs_em.OeZ3Kx&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250317_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1005&akid=8369%2E5257348%2EjFsdJH ]Please chip in a few dollars to help me keep up these efforts
to defend democracy, both at home and abroad.

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1015?c0=q12025_abs_em.sOF3cu&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250317_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1006&akid=8369%2E5257348%2EjFsdJH ]EXPRESS DONATE $10

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1016?c0=q12025_abs_em.yoPhsC&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250317_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1007&akid=8369%2E5257348%2EjFsdJH ]EXPRESS DONATE $25

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1017?c0=q12025_abs_em.rGpVdd&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250317_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1008&akid=8369%2E5257348%2EjFsdJH ]EXPRESS DONATE $50

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1018?c0=q12025_abs_em.zzYphW&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250317_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1009&akid=8369%2E5257348%2EjFsdJH ]EXPRESS DONATE $100

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1019?c0=q12025_abs_em.KUIsEV&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20250317_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1010&akid=8369%2E5257348%2EjFsdJH ]EXPRESS DONATE $250

We can never take for granted our precious, fragile democracy.

Thank you,

Adam

I know what it takes to keep America safe and secure.

| [](http://links.harriganfornc.com/u/click?_t=687026f8d8f7415aa539c5385c376b1f&_m=a4843f6d8f6f45ef9705361fbf27219a&_e=4fN2zZ0nvVKpK7HEFC4yxvq6_gy3YO7MjWPE0wiILenbZuusaRnosL1sIFx8KYW3n5-BgfjuHGdCmoPPTXGrA3h8A3DEjhpc-qkSblagLfDbZ7z0G924T0EM1mgZC9LjPrLNvN1sbco4BEuQdCYW8dxg7Pb6KOyIVfwtz2UbpHZNHn-H12kGXVE9oSed5y5r6JUd6cT5ZXcdWiSUY7rx5-mIwigIJ3uSNMOU3ceomj0izTAkmbBqMel-swo2RHhIZujGZ3AhhI7dPVc0kYHhkh6l-YYG_4t0Dr0XfbzC-4fADI0Vp4eHdoRs_xvRM20ORTTOZZQe0bUx_a5A-c6mnhZ3VklycGPYFSuTzihwRMk%3D)

Patriot,

I am honored to be appointed to the House Armed Services Committee. As a Green Beret who has seen the cost of freedom firsthand, I know what it takes to keep America safe and secure.

Serving on this critical committee, I will work to:

**✅ Rebuild our military strength and restore our Peace Through Strength approach.**

**✅ Ensure our troops have the resources, training, and authorities they need to WIN.**

**✅ Bolster defense manufacturing and protect our supply chains right here at home.**

**✅ Counter China 's military ambitions and maintain America's dominance on the global stage.**

This committee is not just about defense--it's about ensuring American leadership, security, and prosperity for future generations.

**[Click here to join my team and help me Make America Secure Again!](http://links.harriganfornc.com/u/click?_t=687026f8d8f7415aa539c5385c376b1f&_m=a4843f6d8f6f45ef9705361fbf27219a&_e=4fN2zZ0nvVKpK7HEFC4yxvq6_gy3YO7MjWPE0wiILenbZuusaRnosL1sIFx8KYW3n5-BgfjuHGdCmoPPTXGrA3h8A3DEjhpc-qkSblagLfDbZ7z0G924T0EM1mgZC9LjPrLNvN1sbco4BEuQdCYW8XUHcIQA_j3etzz-CI9drnsieP57-5Zmyrhe5Ui8SQs5JV2ICu3OAv2G8oTfWqAMlddtYg7SJnAOu1uwmYauszN-D1BKOsTvq53NZQY_mfvVkVmB84pb1rx2OJ8yB8c-944liZ3D7fmk4flhJdVv4aq2TUMzvFTHx43yknKPu_FlqVNaspzwPU-_L_-Ii109cQ%3D%3D)** Let's get to work!

disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Democrat Coalition Action Fund
body:
Our constituents shouldn’t have to suffer because Republicans want to act like children.

| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104029076/534034850/-2140435400?refcode=header-img&amount=25&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84Mjc1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDZjNzc5OS02ZTAzLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gpQDAmJk8oMnPB-NmKlGxo2ImJvaE2RCCsMhWvxxf4g=)
---
Hi friend, it’s Congresswoman Sarah McBride. The New Dems team said I could reach out because you have what it takes to stand up in critical moments — and this is one of them.

**Yet another GOP politician decided to get his own 15 Minutes of Twitter Fame at my expense.**

At a subcommittee hearing, the Republican chairman referred to me as “Mr. McBride.” And when Rep. Bill Keating defended me, the chair _adjourned the hearing_ to avoid acknowledging his own immaturity. **Republicans are a bunch of overgrown schoolyard bullies.** Our constituents shouldn’t have to suffer because Republicans want to act like children. **This pettiness needs to end.** I was there ready to ask questions on nuclear non-proliferation and US support for our democratic allies in Europe.

**I ran for Congress to roll up my sleeves and do the job.** No matter what they throw at me, I’m 100% focused on delivering for Delawareans. And if that means listening to their childish insults, _so be it._

**[I’m proud to have New Dems’ support at this moment, but we need you with us. clean_body: Our constituents shouldn’t have to suffer because Republicans want to act like children. | = Hi friend, it’s Congresswoman Sarah McBride. The New Dems team said I could reach out because you have what it takes to stand up in critical moments — and this is one of them. Yet another GOP politician decided to get his own 15 Minutes of Twitter Fame at my expense. At a subcommittee hearing, the Republican chairman referred to me as “Mr. McBride.” And when Rep. Bill Keating defended me, the chair adjourned the hearing to avoid acknowledging his own immaturity. Republicans are a bunch of overgrown schoolyard bullies. Our constituents shouldn’t have to suffer because Republicans want to act like children. This pettiness needs to end. I was there ready to ask questions on nuclear non-proliferation and US support for our democratic allies in Europe. I ran for Congress to roll up my sleeves and do the job. No matter what they throw at me, I’m 100% focused on delivering for Delawareans. And if that means listening to their childish insults, so be it. = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Sarah McBride and New Dems will go through immediately: = = = = = Thank you for your support, Sarah McBride = | | Paid for by the New Democrat Coalition Action Fund www.newdemactionfund.com Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 910 17th St NW Suite 1050, Washington, DC 20006 In 2022, NewDems flipped more competitive seats from red-to-blue than any other ideological caucus. * * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Gov. Greg Abbott 's national profile is on the rise**," Dallas Morning News' **Gromer Jeffers, Jr.** -- "From his strategic partnership with President Donald Trump to his headline-grabbing border policies, Gov. **Greg Abbott** is riding a wave of Republican popularity that has raised his national profile and could put a 2028 White House campaign within reach if he wants it.

Few Republican governors have Abbott's national stature, and the Texas governor for the past decade has basked in praise from Trump, who devoted part of his Jan. 20 inaugural speeches to[ lauding Abbott as the "leader of the pack" among the nation's governors](https://substack.com/redirect/9ddf885e-7bfb-48a8-b979-52ba2509c5f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

At the least, Abbott will have the choice to run for president or stay as governor in the nation's most important and prosperous Republican state. Either way, he can strongly influence GOP politics on a national scale.

"That's something that really came to mind when I saw Trump praise Abbott during that second inaugural speech," said**Aaron Kall** , a University of Michigan political scientist and author of the book Debating the Donald.

"Abbott and Trump are similar. A lot of the governor's actions are designed to solicit media attention on a larger, national stage. That suggests the potential for seeking something beyond being governor of Texas, and it will be a wide-open race in 2028," Kall said.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/36b10a80-1080-43e1-8880-1306890ed90e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Abbott is, for now, focused on his future in Texas, his chief political consultant,**Dave Carney** , said.

"The governor has been consistent that he has the second-best job in the country, and he loves what he's doing and he's going to run for reelection," Carney said. "That's what he's focused on, Texas. He told the president when he [Trump] was a candidate that he has no interest in going to Washington."

Even so, speculation about Abbott's future persists.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/36b10a80-1080-43e1-8880-1306890ed90e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Abbott and Trump see eye-to-eye on immigration, border security and reducing government regulation, providing common ground for one of the governor's newest objectives -- federal reimbursement for $11 billion spent on state border-security efforts during the Biden administration. Abbott has made several treks to Washington to rally support for the payout.

Buttering Texas' toast is one benefit to being cozy with Trump. From a political perspective, the Trump-Abbott partnership improves his standing with Trump loyalists and conservative Republicans outside of Texas.

Abbott briefly pondered a presidential run during the 2024 cycle and made a few appearances in battleground states before the presidential campaign season. But he never fully explored a candidacy and backed away from the prospect when Trump emerged as the clear GOP frontrunner.

Term limits set up an open race for the Oval Office in 2028." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/8eaa31ad-a0cc-4177-a8e3-dc9de738812d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

Read today's FREE subscriber email . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . TOP NEWS "Gov. Greg Abbott 's national profile is on the rise," Dallas Morning News' Gromer Jeffers, Jr. -- "From his strategic partnership with President Donald Trump to his headline-grabbing border policies, Gov. Greg Abbott is riding a wave of Republican popularity that has raised his national profile and could put a 2028 White House campaign within reach if he wants it. Few Republican governors have Abbott's national stature, and the Texas governor for the past decade has basked in praise from Trump, who devoted part of his Jan. 20 inaugural speeches to. At the least, Abbott will have the choice to run for president or stay as governor in the nation's most important and prosperous Republican state. Either way, he can strongly influence GOP politics on a national scale. "That's something that really came to mind when I saw Trump praise Abbott during that second inaugural speech," saidAaron Kall , a University of Michigan political scientist and author of the book Debating the Donald. "Abbott and Trump are similar. A lot of the governor's actions are designed to solicit media attention on a larger, national stage. That suggests the potential for seeking something beyond being governor of Texas, and it will be a wide-open race in 2028," Kall said. Abbott is, for now, focused on his future in Texas, his chief political consultant,Dave Carney , said. "The governor has been consistent that he has the second-best job in the country, and he loves what he's doing and he's going to run for reelection," Carney said. "That's what he's focused on, Texas. He told the president when he [Trump] was a candidate that he has no interest in going to Washington." Even so, speculation about Abbott's future persists. Abbott and Trump see eye-to-eye on immigration, border security and reducing government regulation, providing common ground for one of the governor's newest objectives -- federal reimbursement for $11 billion spent on state border-security efforts during the Biden administration. Abbott has made several treks to Washington to rally support for the payout. Buttering Texas' toast is one benefit to being cozy with Trump. From a political perspective, the Trump-Abbott partnership improves his standing with Trump loyalists and conservative Republicans outside of Texas. Abbott briefly pondered a presidential run during the 2024 cycle and made a few appearances in battleground states before the presidential campaign season. But he never fully explored a candidacy and backed away from the prospect when Trump emerged as the clear GOP frontrunner. Term limits set up an open race for the Oval Office in 2028." ### "What did you do last week?"
Mar 17
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=159281826&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU5MjgxODI2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0MjI0NTQwNCwiZXhwIjoxNzQ0ODM3NDA0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzA3Mjc4MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.FMYO7BwLxqw3Lu9NXfaSCn55WjsLfdF7YmzDoGsaqr8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/politibrawl/p/doge-officer-confronts-federal-employee?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

The DOGE Patrol _**[was on the scene at a gathering of federal workers](https://substack.com/redirect/43ce7b5f-b1be-4388-a0c2-1ef1a548b5dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and other Democrats in Washington D.C., Friday, who were protesting DOGE and Elon Musk's efforts to make the federal government lean and efficient.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/43ce7b5f-b1be-4388-a0c2-1ef1a548b5dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/43ce7b5f-b1be-4388-a0c2-1ef1a548b5dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
I geared up in my blue DOGE shirt, hat and aviator sunglasses as soon as I heard there were hundreds of government employees skipping work on a Friday afternoon, standing in front of the Capitol Building to yell at Trump and Elon.
**["What did you do last week?"](https://substack.com/redirect/43ce7b5f-b1be-4388-a0c2-1ef1a548b5dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** was my question to these people, echoing Elon Musk's straightforward email to federal workers asking for a 5 bullet point list of what they worked on. In theory it should be an easy question to answer, especially if you are a productive and active worker. And being a taxpayer myself meant that I paid these bureaucrats salaries with my hard-earned tax dollars. They could at least do me the curtsey of answering a simple and fair question.
"You fucking piece of shit!!!" one woman shouted in my face, and really, her response echoed the sentiment of almost everybody's response.
# Pick Up Your DOGE Merch Now!
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/20302954-512a-4f72-9647-5a02d194c038?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
"I worked,... I filed papers," another federal employee told me, confused by the question.
"But what did you actually accomplish that was important?" I asked for clarity.
"Nothing... I worked!" he snapped, frustrated by what I thought would be an easy question.
**[I quickly noticed sort of a mob forming around me](https://substack.com/redirect/43ce7b5f-b1be-4388-a0c2-1ef1a548b5dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** as I stood there, wearing my DOGE uniform and white Fila slides. The shouting got louder, ruder and more hostile.
"We don't want nazis here!!!" one older woman berated me. Not to get on a tangent, but I don't remember the nazis being big supporters of small government. They amassed a huge bureaucracy, full of zealots who worshipped the state, mobilized to serve the National Socialist cause.
As the mob grew more enraged by my meme t-shirt I realized we weren't going to have any more meaningful interactions and should probably leave before a nut job did something crazy.
Check out the video to see all hell break loose because of a guy in a t-shirt asking a really easy question:
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/43ce7b5f-b1be-4388-a0c2-1ef1a548b5dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
| | POLL
---
Do you support what DOGE is doing to the federal government?

clean_body: Peter, Donald Trump and his Republican allies are hoarding all the gold—giving massive tax breaks to billionaires and corporate special interests while working families get left with nothing but empty promises. This St. Patrick’s Day, let’s change that. Instead of letting Trump’s friends keep all the treasure, let’s build a future where the pot of gold goes to everyday Americans—the folks who work hard, play by the rules, and just want a fair shot. = Kim is fighting every day in Congress to make sure Washington works for YOU. That means protecting Social Security and Medicare, lowering healthcare costs, and standing up to the extremists trying to take us backwards. And while the work to find the pot of gold at the end of the rainbow won’t be easy, with your support, we can keep fighting for a fair economy where everyone gets a fair shake—not just those at the top. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you 🍀, Kim Schrier for Congress Rep. * * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Gov. Greg Abbott 's national profile is on the rise**," Dallas Morning News' **Gromer Jeffers, Jr.** -- "From his strategic partnership with President Donald Trump to his headline-grabbing border policies, Gov. **Greg Abbott** is riding a wave of Republican popularity that has raised his national profile and could put a 2028 White House campaign within reach if he wants it.

Few Republican governors have Abbott's national stature, and the Texas governor for the past decade has basked in praise from Trump, who devoted part of his Jan. 20 inaugural speeches to[ lauding Abbott as the "leader of the pack" among the nation's governors](https://substack.com/redirect/2424d004-840e-46ee-8a7d-977017e947d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

At the least, Abbott will have the choice to run for president or stay as governor in the nation's most important and prosperous Republican state. Either way, he can strongly influence GOP politics on a national scale.

"That's something that really came to mind when I saw Trump praise Abbott during that second inaugural speech," said**Aaron Kall** , a University of Michigan political scientist and author of the book Debating the Donald.

"Abbott and Trump are similar. A lot of the governor's actions are designed to solicit media attention on a larger, national stage. That suggests the potential for seeking something beyond being governor of Texas, and it will be a wide-open race in 2028," Kall said.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/e95511ec-d8c1-4172-94f3-d813438e6dd6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Abbott is, for now, focused on his future in Texas, his chief political consultant,**Dave Carney** , said.

"The governor has been consistent that he has the second-best job in the country, and he loves what he's doing and he's going to run for reelection," Carney said. "That's what he's focused on, Texas. He told the president when he [Trump] was a candidate that he has no interest in going to Washington."

Even so, speculation about Abbott's future persists.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/e95511ec-d8c1-4172-94f3-d813438e6dd6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Abbott and Trump see eye-to-eye on immigration, border security and reducing government regulation, providing common ground for one of the governor's newest objectives -- federal reimbursement for $11 billion spent on state border-security efforts during the Biden administration. Abbott has made several treks to Washington to rally support for the payout.

Buttering Texas' toast is one benefit to being cozy with Trump. From a political perspective, the Trump-Abbott partnership improves his standing with Trump loyalists and conservative Republicans outside of Texas.

Abbott briefly pondered a presidential run during the 2024 cycle and made a few appearances in battleground states before the presidential campaign season. But he never fully explored a candidacy and backed away from the prospect when Trump emerged as the clear GOP frontrunner.

Term limits set up an open race for the Oval Office in 2028." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/a993dcaa-35de-4b1a-9890-af0ad2bb7ed9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

Read today's FREE subscriber email . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . TOP NEWS "Gov. Greg Abbott 's national profile is on the rise," Dallas Morning News' Gromer Jeffers, Jr. -- "From his strategic partnership with President Donald Trump to his headline-grabbing border policies, Gov. Greg Abbott is riding a wave of Republican popularity that has raised his national profile and could put a 2028 White House campaign within reach if he wants it. Few Republican governors have Abbott's national stature, and the Texas governor for the past decade has basked in praise from Trump, who devoted part of his Jan. 20 inaugural speeches to. At the least, Abbott will have the choice to run for president or stay as governor in the nation's most important and prosperous Republican state. Either way, he can strongly influence GOP politics on a national scale. "That's something that really came to mind when I saw Trump praise Abbott during that second inaugural speech," saidAaron Kall , a University of Michigan political scientist and author of the book Debating the Donald. "Abbott and Trump are similar. A lot of the governor's actions are designed to solicit media attention on a larger, national stage. That suggests the potential for seeking something beyond being governor of Texas, and it will be a wide-open race in 2028," Kall said. Abbott is, for now, focused on his future in Texas, his chief political consultant,Dave Carney , said. "The governor has been consistent that he has the second-best job in the country, and he loves what he's doing and he's going to run for reelection," Carney said. "That's what he's focused on, Texas. He told the president when he [Trump] was a candidate that he has no interest in going to Washington." Even so, speculation about Abbott's future persists. Abbott and Trump see eye-to-eye on immigration, border security and reducing government regulation, providing common ground for one of the governor's newest objectives -- federal reimbursement for $11 billion spent on state border-security efforts during the Biden administration. Abbott has made several treks to Washington to rally support for the payout. Buttering Texas' toast is one benefit to being cozy with Trump. From a political perspective, the Trump-Abbott partnership improves his standing with Trump loyalists and conservative Republicans outside of Texas. Abbott briefly pondered a presidential run during the 2024 cycle and made a few appearances in battleground states before the presidential campaign season. But he never fully explored a candidacy and backed away from the prospect when Trump emerged as the clear GOP frontrunner. Term limits set up an open race for the Oval Office in 2028." * **TOP NEWS**

“**Gov. Greg Abbott’s national profile is on the rise** ,” Dallas Morning News' **Gromer Jeffers, Jr.** — “From his strategic partnership with President Donald Trump to his headline-grabbing border policies, Gov. **Greg Abbott** is riding a wave of Republican popularity that has raised his national profile and could put a 2028 White House campaign within reach if he wants it.

Few Republican governors have Abbott’s national stature, and the Texas governor for the past decade has basked in praise from Trump, who devoted part of his Jan. 20 inaugural speeches to[ lauding Abbott as the “leader of the pack” among the nation’s governors](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_kt4zZ7BA9X6VHUBegwwAunJJGn_vPjtYfAxIm74IoQWQKVPqQh0asHaeA8nds-9OrYqE0dix9SYVhKLl49H1ZDH3Qlg2NK3ReqSVie2addnSI6elPJRtjC0AzGuJcy3zgDBTGXgbzzVHNoLzLvgh7lNj5KVDh2Aj9puWUgnvse_g79HnUsM6tDOl9_-U3XH-0Gr21mQmNww-OMFxyBXI1Ja51yOQV62tkuIopfxfBzgeHm5nJt_O-q15Z8p9VG5mMr2RAkEQkkf4e4O0dTL2CyBB41mBPy3uFbeJHVaQNb-QtF5ro86B_6_fqtRKluBnf_snXfR4nU8J97BV28jKiiS39ZeMnfmYRpC8EodCKi57_oD2BFRvxiMXNQeRaMxyO-YM0NsyBX6jz3YDO1JtCQTeglC6Q-FaKLlZonld57WScSZsVIPBI2ojoqO_hORjUT_s0m6pYOJSPdMW4xkSrPxN-RXtE13Z-14z2LBPnjPKyr7Fup4Lb7hNwd_T05Sws/4ev/uNZcSIoMT3eNvsoM6e29Nw/h10/h001.XUViMuyySzWQIQa9xFYVa4zVun38nmqVnRbVoVw7Zu8).

At the least, Abbott will have the choice to run for president or stay as governor in the nation’s most important and prosperous Republican state. Either way, he can strongly influence GOP politics on a national scale.

“That’s something that really came to mind when I saw Trump praise Abbott during that second inaugural speech,” said**Aaron Kall** , a University of Michigan political scientist and author of the book Debating the Donald.

“Abbott and Trump are similar. A lot of the governor’s actions are designed to solicit media attention on a larger, national stage. That suggests the potential for seeking something beyond being governor of Texas, and it will be a wide-open race in 2028,” Kall said.
» [BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.IpGzAqnc3m6dMmKQdod4OdZdsfX-sjnRfDuEmaXW-5lN-IqpP1xu2_Ygevf9CBs8iGkDwdDuKv-OIdRCcryDDgoqnwbBfsyGSk_4VJrOSCvmgxZEZTEXv72w0U68JXHpny88FhFoE4mM3a9phL8bbMMIYy_3u36qnI_bTpQANJILa36TbJjqqV8JqAdKkZOhnepOidB_jFwpcnGGT658ouU4L0GzZmmaBbkxUZb1gdqlfPF35ulUU6pjDzCmz8jEME72v2vjrbWyuU-iDmDpTKhKQo76ualh2exX_84JyD6fyHY5woxJ698XrFLma7K0BWCKjj0uDqn-6PC5pL4xR9nur229S-ntYiDi7R7zHB0UP0n2iL5lUGS_mszrbRGXHSEUst_Z90HM3FJB_CI5gg/4ev/uNZcSIoMT3eNvsoM6e29Nw/h11/h001.YHBKBGpJ7ilAiE7r3A1mcyXuNpxC4ciYgGP_Rt-102w)
Abbott is, for now, focused on his future in Texas, his chief political consultant,**Dave Carney** , said.

“The governor has been consistent that he has the second-best job in the country, and he loves what he’s doing and he’s going to run for reelection,” Carney said. “That’s what he’s focused on, Texas. He told the president when he [Trump] was a candidate that he has no interest in going to Washington.”

Even so, speculation about Abbott’s future persists.
» [BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.IpGzAqnc3m6dMmKQdod4OdZdsfX-sjnRfDuEmaXW-5lN-IqpP1xu2_Ygevf9CBs8iGkDwdDuKv-OIdRCcryDDgoqnwbBfsyGSk_4VJrOSCvmgxZEZTEXv72w0U68JXHpny88FhFoE4mM3a9phL8bbMMIYy_3u36qnI_bTpQANJILa36TbJjqqV8JqAdKkZOhnepOidB_jFwpcnGGT658ouU4L0GzZmmaBbkxUZb1gdqlfPF35ulUU6pjDzCmz8jEME72v2vjrbWyuU-iDmDpTKhKQo76ualh2exX_84JyD6fyHY5woxJ698XrFLma7K0BWCKjj0uDqn-6PC5pL4xRyRFBLwEHrGU-BNfx61tMLyu_UOjypm0X3TcoPUROU1QRTUIIDkh6TrLkTtWRVauLQ/4ev/uNZcSIoMT3eNvsoM6e29Nw/h12/h001.XQzRXX4e5zJ0s4IulirF_IOSfhx1aVT-ExeKTvnUnZo)
Abbott and Trump see eye-to-eye on immigration, border security and reducing government regulation, providing common ground for one of the governor’s newest objectives — federal reimbursement for $11 billion spent on state border-security efforts during the Biden administration. Abbott has made several treks to Washington to rally support for the payout.

Buttering Texas’ toast is one benefit to being cozy with Trump. From a political perspective, the Trump-Abbott partnership improves his standing with Trump loyalists and conservative Republicans outside of Texas.

Abbott briefly pondered a presidential run during the 2024 cycle and made a few appearances in battleground states before the presidential campaign season. But he never fully explored a candidacy and backed away from the prospect when Trump emerged as the clear GOP frontrunner.

Term limits set up an open race for the Oval Office in 2028." [DMN](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOQPXJLmqZGCIlkWp25_d_kt4zZ7BA9X6VHUBegwwAunNIvhSGCCCbZ_gBZLf49cm3IbPfW8PbAFi_YVfDBrtqEuWoPTSt2BEZpQpSwafMkjQbgCNPUm-7cUtzMi6Z3mrCkfTc8gAGh0hjjq002Zl87taK1tG50u01QlPhJteD-XJ1xpa5unb9kRw1R8BxRsclUyJ6HLwbEMQ7pfNi_3cuNwqbQWyNf9pikCqMpXYqVr1pyBrwWPBisl90bkKirAVLthw-_XdBdqkjj62QZbtewG4RROxskwEWMO5MM71J4Dw3U2UMDqFPIBwQuxcD0yiGEBYq7abH7TvRUemtxK6qIXYKHqNpo87dKnHKToztCynsj9YWup1OLKoU92Nj-SHVdIZXqkIPG4XpGVUwQxSMQC7Be3OKxvQOPuaP5DnxV_w0i4A4LnZyqyEjO4t5fKVEZ7P5zbYkBNO_uMHUt8_5Zfh5BTTDHbV8HWj-HO8veUlkdvDCL9tx9-yE0YZFkbetDGgydZTYYukxs4M9Aze5Q/4ev/uNZcSIoMT3eNvsoM6e29Nw/h13/h001.849G4QOzgIIrh25nF_Px2LJ1dPa6-PnZmOMKNBD6wAQ) ($)

Read today's FREE subscriber email . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . | TOP NEWS “Gov. Greg Abbott’s national profile is on the rise ,” Dallas Morning News' Gromer Jeffers, Jr. — “From his strategic partnership with President Donald Trump to his headline-grabbing border policies, Gov. Greg Abbott is riding a wave of Republican popularity that has raised his national profile and could put a 2028 White House campaign within reach if he wants it. Few Republican governors have Abbott’s national stature, and the Texas governor for the past decade has basked in praise from Trump, who devoted part of his Jan. 20 inaugural speeches to. At the least, Abbott will have the choice to run for president or stay as governor in the nation’s most important and prosperous Republican state. Either way, he can strongly influence GOP politics on a national scale. “That’s something that really came to mind when I saw Trump praise Abbott during that second inaugural speech,” saidAaron Kall , a University of Michigan political scientist and author of the book Debating the Donald. “Abbott and Trump are similar. A lot of the governor’s actions are designed to solicit media attention on a larger, national stage. That suggests the potential for seeking something beyond being governor of Texas, and it will be a wide-open race in 2028,” Kall said. » Abbott is, for now, focused on his future in Texas, his chief political consultant,Dave Carney , said. “The governor has been consistent that he has the second-best job in the country, and he loves what he’s doing and he’s going to run for reelection,” Carney said. “That’s what he’s focused on, Texas. He told the president when he [Trump] was a candidate that he has no interest in going to Washington.” Even so, speculation about Abbott’s future persists. » Abbott and Trump see eye-to-eye on immigration, border security and reducing government regulation, providing common ground for one of the governor’s newest objectives — federal reimbursement for $11 billion spent on state border-security efforts during the Biden administration. Abbott has made several treks to Washington to rally support for the payout. Buttering Texas’ toast is one benefit to being cozy with Trump. From a political perspective, the Trump-Abbott partnership improves his standing with Trump loyalists and conservative Republicans outside of Texas. Abbott briefly pondered a presidential run during the 2024 cycle and made a few appearances in battleground states before the presidential campaign season. But he never fully explored a candidacy and backed away from the prospect when Trump emerged as the clear GOP frontrunner. Term limits set up an open race for the Oval Office in 2028." name: Jason Crow
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Congress [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/logoJC%20(1).png]
Hi team, I wanted to reach out to share a huge thank you for the birthday wishes, and yes, the campaign contributions.

Representing Coloradans is an honor of a lifetime, and I don’t take this privilege for granted.

I’m in this fight because there is still so much that needs to get done — and my pledge in this new year is to keep up this important work.

We need to defend our democracy.

We need to protect our rights.

We need to lower costs for working families and level the playing field.

And we need to stand up to Donald Trump.

My time in combat taught me to never back down from a fight, and I don’t intend to stop now. Ushering in a new generation of servant leadership starts with us.

I’m deeply grateful for your support, it means the world to me.

Thank you!

— Jason

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-mar25]
Jason Crow for Congress
8547 E Arapahoe Road, STE J-543
Greenwood Village, CO 80112
United States
www.jasoncrowforcongress.com [https://www.jasoncrowforcongress.com/] | info@jasoncrowforcongress.com [info@jasoncrowforcongress.com]
Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress

name: Chrissy Houlahan
subject: What I’m doing to stop the madness
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chrissy Houlahan for Congress
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104029822/534037472/-1072566757?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNWMyODBjYS03MjAzLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yvx78evvHtgd_tkemCtTE3jD6lytAcTHZ_QFPVR3TPM=&emci=caec0c5b-b8fa-ef11-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=a5c280ca-7203-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=21278485)
---
It’s been hard to grapple with how little structural power Democrats have in Washington right now; Republicans have full control of the House, the Senate, and the White House. Democrats can’t force a vote on bills (like one to remove Musk from our government payment systems) or force Administration officials (like Secretary of State Marco Rubio) to get back to us (after I contacted his office pleading my case for the preservation of USAID).
But! Democrats **are** fighting back. In the unrelenting shock-and-awe dominance of the press, and the pace at which President Trump and Elon Musk are creating news, I understand why that’s not always breaking through. I’m speaking out on social media, on TV, in the traditional press, and in speeches on the floor of the House.
What else am I doing?
* I’m helping to recruit candidates who can help us take back the House and be a check on President Trump and his allies. **[It's working.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104029823/534037474/640641795?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNWMyODBjYS03MjAzLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yvx78evvHtgd_tkemCtTE3jD6lytAcTHZ_QFPVR3TPM=&emci=caec0c5b-b8fa-ef11-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=a5c280ca-7203-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=21278485)**
* I found Secretary Rubio in Munich at the Munich Security Conference and talked to him directly about the importance of USAID.
* I’m joining amicus brief (aka friend of the court) filings in support of cases filed against the Trump Administration.
* Behind the scenes (because doing so publicly will backfire), I’m urging my Republican colleagues to speak up. As one of the most bipartisan members of Congress, I know I have their ear. My bullhorn is smaller than Trump's or Musk’s but it’s not nothing.
* I cosponsored the Taxpayer Data Protection Act (to force Musk out of our systems) and have called on Treasury Secretary Bessent to explain and justify Musk’s behavior.

This feels like a David and Goliath moment. While this fight is going to be longer than that one, I like our odds. Thank you for supporting our work.
| | [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104029824/534037475/685235109?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1105-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNWMyODBjYS03MjAzLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yvx78evvHtgd_tkemCtTE3jD6lytAcTHZ_QFPVR3TPM=)
---
There’s work to be done,
Chrissy
| Chrissy Houlahan for Congress
P.O. Box 222
Devon, PA 19333
United States Emails are a crucial way for us to stay in touch with our top supporters; however, we understand if you need a break from political emails right now. Do you want to join an exclusive evening at Seth Koslow’s favorite cigar bar? | | Dear Friends,

**Do you want to join an exclusive evening at Seth Koslow’s favorite cigar bar?**

Join Seth on March 31st from 6-8 PM for an unforgettable evening at **The General Cigar Bar** in East Meadow. Enjoy great company, a relaxed setting, and meaningful conversations about the future of Nassau County.

[**Click here to RSVP and support Seth’s campaign for Nassau County Executive. Space is limited, so be sure to reserve your spot today!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104029845/534037502/911216724?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTYyMTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYTNjMjgwY2EtNzIwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ftR5gVcjoOAUZcYTMULE_zK9HZyG-jQsCcLJRxF6y3E=&emci=af628da8-4f03-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=a3c280ca-7203-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=17551891)
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104029846/534037503/911216724?nvep=&hmac=&emci=e89a76f9-91fa-ef11-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=16908693&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTYyMTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYTNjMjgwY2EtNzIwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ftR5gVcjoOAUZcYTMULE_zK9HZyG-jQsCcLJRxF6y3E=)
As Nassau County Executive, Seth has five critical steps to move the County forward!

**Cut Taxes** by ending government fraud, waste & abuse, and costly party-driven favoritism to lower taxes. Auditing all political jobs in the County then eliminating all unnecessary political jobs.

**Protect Homes, Businesses & Families** with real law enforcement upgrades. Increasing overall Nassau Police enrollment by 10% and rebuilding our detective department.

**Replace the Failed Assessment System** to make the property tax rates fair for everyone in Nassau County. Transferring assessment responsibility to the towns and cities for fairer valuations and make challenging assessments simpler to help eliminate the need for lawyers.

**Improve our Quality of Life & Affordability** through establishing an Entrepreneurial Ombudsman to cut business red-tape and approval times, helping to bring businesses to Nassau, creating jobs and tax revenues.

**Plan Nassau’s Future** by establishing a Nassau Master Plan Commission to create a development roadmap for the future.

[**Click here to reserve your ticket and together we can move Nassau County forward one point at a time!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104029847/534037504/911216724?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTYyMTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYTNjMjgwY2EtNzIwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ftR5gVcjoOAUZcYTMULE_zK9HZyG-jQsCcLJRxF6y3E=&emci=af628da8-4f03-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=a3c280ca-7203-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=17551891)

Thank you for your time and support. See you on March 31st!

Best regards,
Team Koslow

Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in.

Convicted felon Donald Trump and his MAGA sycophants are reshaping our national climate research so that it supports his narrative that climate change is GOOD for us, not bad.
While climate change [evidence](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.xqm-apKpWqGuZCDDu9wrxbiIja_rNRQQtrBF0FOiuSBjC3Z6EvIkD468T8hPbVSCHZ9KeO7gHg8YOsoHFn56Z3eNRI8hSQ3c3Rv1WXhVZUXkPYc3efGKmgTnjOr_oPyLyAuRK1Mgtcrlwu_-yyWnCPfJy0KvlmWQ4peYnaE42zkItmXB9r4gOBOsMUar8R48XHshqnDf3-iHGVBETdyfyqvzzblh6YQtd5nwx43FUPeNAROAqULfKlV1fDkmNRNLwU02zlvEVj7B4GpMGqQ6vImCCAmKw_u5SosT3x1xPN8jijs9vFEB_Zi7yVndeSJs7BhcS4-glkzR-3bKu35_rZuBEg6GXuI5jgQttgIESFlAiQOs2gn76QsBF84YqUBkPhowOt9MVSi1lzh_QBo-8tyZUxyK7R4yiFx6VByI6Te7TR4LiSdd81enBpMqaIUa5ipU2Bta9nSeeg7J8bRaSw/4ev/CxItGuN8R-Of9W53OcgjUQ/h2/h001.8SBhk875-c_js-pYc7t3dlaI_sOkK71Uvh45kz8FJss) has grown stronger, the Trump regime, enabled by MAGA Republicans at every level of government, is waging a full-scale battle against climate science, and the consequences would be catastrophic for the entire world.

name: Final Notice (via VPP)
subject: FWD: Bernie Sanders vs. TRUMP
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
The GOP is FURIOUS...
| | **THIS IS AN AUTOMATED MESSAGE FOR YOU** We’re reaching to Democrats who have not added their signature to our time-sensitive letter to Bernie Sanders.

Bernie Sanders is touring the nation, mobilizing Democrats to take on the fight against Trump... __and the GOP is furious.__

**_It’s absolutely critical we stand with him!_**

[**We have limited spots left. Please confirm your signature is submitted before we close this letter at midnight:**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/88265?t=6&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV) | [**ADD YOUR NAME WHILE YOU CAN: THANK SENATOR BERNIE SANDERS FOR LEADING THE FIGHT AGAINST TRUMP »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/88265?t=7&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV)
---
Thank you,
The Voter Protection Project
**\- - - - - Begin Forwarded Message - - - - -**
FROM: “How to fight Trump” (via VPP)
TO: <dpwillis67@gmail.com>
Subject: Bernie Sanders is LEADING the fight >>
**🚨 BREAKING: DEMOCRATS FIGHT BACK! 🚨**
[](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/88265?t=8&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV)
Bernie Sanders is LEADING the charge to take on Trump and save our Democracy. We’re _THRILLED!_

**Trump and MAGA Republicans are already attacking him relentlessly, so we’re sending Sen. Bernie Sanders a MASSIVE letter thanking him for all his amazing work!**

[**You’ve been PERSONALLY selected to sign, friend, so please don’t let us down: Add your name to thank Senator Sanders now! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/88265?t=9&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV)
[**Donate $3 to sign the card now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/88265?t=10&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV)
---
TO: Sen. Bernie Sanders
FROM: A proud Democrat
Subject: Thank you. **Dear Senator Bernie Sanders,**

__Thank you for all your hard work leading the Anti-Trump resistance!__

It’s easy to feel helpless with MAGA Republicans in charge of the House, Senate, and White House, but you keep us hopeful that we can fight back.

**I know it isn’t easy fighting off Trump and the GOP’s escalating threats to take you down, so please know that Democrats _across the country_ stand with you.**

Your leadership is absolutely critical right now, and we hope more Democrats follow your lead.

[**I can’t thank you enough for all of your work to stop Donald Trump and save our Democracy! So I’m signing this letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to show my gratitude and to keep electing incredible Democrats who will save our democracy from MAGA extremists like Donald Trump. »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/88265?t=11&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/88265?t=12&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV)
---
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
\- The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=13&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=19309%2E878179%2ENq9NfV)

name: Emerge HQ
subject: Unprecedented threats
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emerge America
body:
With Donald Trump’s return to power, women across the country are facing unprecedented threats to accessing lifesaving reproductive healthcare services.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/104030278/534039215/-245045952?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-03-17_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyYmY5ODlhLTczMDMtZjAxMS05MGNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=bgYAskERZP6FXKRL7MaNTue7NMflC9doOmHED_b66Rw=)
---
| Peter,

With Donald Trump’s return to power, women across the country are facing unprecedented threats to accessing lifesaving reproductive healthcare services. Since taking office, his administration has moved swiftly to undermine reproductive freedom in the U.S. and across the world: **Restricting abortion access.** The Trump administration directed the Department of Justice to dismiss lawsuits that protect emergency abortion care, endangering women’s health nationwide. **Defunding essential services.** Efforts are currently underway to cut funding for organizations that provide abortion services, such as Planned Parenthood, jeopardizing access to care for millions of women. **Reinstating the Global Gag Rule.** This policy blocks U.S. aid to international organizations that provide or discuss abortion services, leading to reduced access to contraception and increased maternal mortality globally. These actions are part of a broader agenda aimed at rolling back the clock and undoing decades of progress toward securing gender equality. Now more than ever, we need to elect strong Democratic women who understand their communities' needs and will fight to defend our rights from these attacks.

[_**Will you help us fight back by activating your 2025 Emerge membership and renewing your support today?**_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/104030279/534039216/-245045952?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-03-17_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQyYmY5ODlhLTczMDMtZjAxMS05MGNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=bgYAskERZP6FXKRL7MaNTue7NMflC9doOmHED_b66Rw=)
---

Emerge HQ

Now more than ever, we need to elect strong Democratic women who understand their communities' needs and will fight to defend our rights from these attacks. = | | | = Thank you for standing with us, Emerge HQ * | | = Paid for by Emerge America Emerge America 4 Embarcadero Center Suite 1400 San Francisco, CA 94111 United States

subject: A quick moment of joy

body:
#
We’re going to be real with you: there’s a lot of negative news right now. It’s overwhelming and it sucks.
We wanted to take a quick break from that to hopefully bring you just a moment of joy by sharing some photos Beto took on his hike on Saturday with his dogs:
We hope this brought a quick moment of positivity to your inbox.
