name: Senator Jennifer Boysko
subject: Boysko Bulletin: Join Me at Our Town Halls 3/18 and 3/24
body:
| | |
---
**Boysko Bulletin:****Join Me at Our Town Halls 3/18 and 3/24**
---
Dear Peter, It’s March - we recognize women in two different ways. Women’s History Month, observed every March, is a celebration of women’s contributions to history, culture and society and has been observed annually since 1987. [Equal Pay Day 2025 is on March 25](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033746/534051156/-1880268935?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556) and marks the current state of the gender pay gap for women: 83% for full-time, year-round workers and 75% for all workers (including part-time and seasonal). March 25 is the day when women’s pay including the whole prior year’s earnings equals that of what men earned in the prior year. That’s confusing and disturbing - no one should earn less because they are a woman or a minority. The Governor has the opportunity to sign my bill requiring that hiring notices include salary or wage ranges and that salary history isn’t used in hiring - steps that would narrow the wage gap. Please join me for a town hall - this Tuesday in Reston and next Monday in McLean. We will discuss the legislative session and what’s happening with the federal government. There are resources for federal workers and immigrants included in this newsletter.
---
---
---
We await the Governor’s signature on my legislation - he has until midnight on March 24th to sign, amend or veto. We will return to Richmond on April 2 to vote on his actions. I look forward to meeting with you soon. Kindest regards,
---
---
Jennifer B. BoyskoMember, Senate of VirginiaRepresenting the newly created 38th Senate District making up the northernmost portions of Fairfax County (Great Falls, Herndon, McLean, Reston, WolfTrap, parts of unincorporated Vienna and a slice of Falls Church) PS If you would like to support my work, please visit [my fundraising page](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033747/534051157/1617457950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=). **Photo Gallery**
---
---
---
Delegate Sewell and I held a press conference today at a small business, asking the Governor to sign paid family and medical leave legislation. Virginians would have the opportunity to take up to 12 weeks of paid leave in the event of a serious personal illness, to care for a family member with a serious personal illness or to care for a new baby.
---
---
---
---
Recognizing the wonderful volunteers of the Dranesville District Democrats
---
---
---
---
Presenting the impact and importance of federal funding for Virginia's Transportation Infrastructure at the Environmental and Energy Institute's Congressional Camp - prep for the 2026 reauthorization of the federal Surface Transportation Bill
---
**Legislative Updates** **[Senator Boysko’s 2025 Legislative Slate - Awaiting the Governor’s Signature ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033748/534051158/17978256?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556)** **SJ247 Constitutional amendment; fundamental right to reproductive freedom (first reference):** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1002 Campaign finance; prohibited personal use of campaign funds; complaints, hearings, civil penalty, and advisory opinions:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1004 Trailer franchise requirements; applicability to certain trailers** : Passed the General Assembly! **SB1005 Services for sexual assault patients; provision of information for sexual assault patients; Task Force on Services for Survivors of Sexual Assault; work group; report** : Passed the General Assembly! **SB1013 Affirmative defense or reduced penalty for a neurocognitive disorder or intellectual or developmental disability** : Passed the General Assembly! **SB1122 Paid family and medical leave insurance program; notice requirements; civil action** : Passed the Senate; failed in the House after it was brought to the Floor as a revenue bill requiring more than a simple majority vote. While frustrating, I am glad that Delegate Sewell's identical bill passed in both the House and the Senate. **SB1123 Extreme Weather Relief Act established:** passed by in the Senate Agriculture, Conservation and Natural Resources Committee with a letter for further study **SB1124 Board of Education; driver education programs; computer-based driver education courses; requirements** : Passed the General Assembly! **SB1125 Department of Wildlife Resources; propagation of wildlife without a permit unlawful; premature separation; hybridization:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1126 Students with individualized education programs or Section 504 Plans; emergency protocol and guide:** killed in the full Senate Education and Health Committee although I am pleased that Delegate Cohen’s version passed the General Assembly **SB1127 Animal testing facilities; public notification:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1129 National Teacher Certification Incentive Reward Program and Fund; eligibility; incentive grant awards:** reported from the Senate Education and Health Committee and killed in the Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee. **SB1132 Prohibiting employer seeking wage or salary history of prospective employees; wage or salary range transparency; cause of action:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1133 Restaurants and catering operations; notice of food containing pork products; civil penalty:** Passed the Senate; killed in the House Health Subcommittee of the Committee on Health and Human Services **SB1134 Storage of firearms in a residence where a minor or person prohibited from possessing a firearm is present; penalty:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1135 Board of Pharmacy; regulation of COMP360:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1136 Local anti-rent gouging authority; civil penalty:** killed by the Senate General Laws Committee **SB1137 Office of Data Governance and Analytics; oversight; Chief Data Officer; duties:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1237 Department of Medical Assistance Services; appeals of agency determinations (Admin Bill):** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1483 Regulated hemp product retail facility registration; fee:** passed the Senate; killed by the Agriculture Subcommittee of the House Agriculture, Chesapeake and Natural Resources Committee **Also Passed 17 Commendations! The Surface Transportation bill is reauthorized every five years. Federal funding makes up 17% of [Virginia’s transportation budget](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033750/534051160/1331453920?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556). Funding for our highways, bridges, bike and pedestrian paths, and transit all depend on federal funding. For as long as I’ve been serving on the Senate Transportation Committee, the Northern Virginia Transportation Authority, and the Metropolitan Washington Council of Governments Transportation Planning Board, we have had more project proposals than available funding. Virginia is fortunate that we have systems in place to evaluate and weigh projects to ensure the greatest benefit from every transportation dollar spent.

| | **Constituent Services** In 2024, there were more than 28,000 federal jobs in Fairfax County, with 78,800 Fairfax County residents working for the federal government. Serious attempts to move the federal government out of our region and to continue with layoffs will have a significant impact on our economy. Of course, the impacts are most serious for the employees who have been threatened with layoffs or fired. I am so sorry for what is happening. I’ve included resources in this newsletter and you are welcome to email me. Federal workers laid off or furloughed by the new federal administration are encouraged to reach out to their federal legislators - [Senator Warner](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033751/534051161/1384386641?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556), [Senator Kaine](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033752/534051162/1707660458?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556) and your [congressional representative](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033753/534051169/-591754513?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556). Fairfax County has created a webpage with a variety of resources that can help former federal employees, contractors and anyone else affected by job loss. The site includes suggestions for federal workers seeking to rewrite their resumes for the private sector, plus region-by-region links to major employers’ corporate career pages and links to region-specific job searches on Indeed and LinkedIn. **Additional Resources for Federal Employees Facing Unemployment:**
* NOVA New Employment, Exploration and Transition (NOVAnext), is a new program to help recently unemployed federal workers and federal contractors transition to their next careers. The first in the region to provide new, dedicated training funds for unemployed federal workers and federal contractors, NOVA is committing up to $1 million in scholarship aid to allow approved participants to enroll in one of 14 courses at no charge and access NOVA-sponsored online workshops on resume-writing and job search techniques. Many classes prepare students for industry-recognized certifications in specific IT specialties, program management and human resources. To be eligible, federal workers and federal contract workers must have been laid off on or after February 1, 2025, reside within the NOVA service area, meet a residency requirement and fill out an online application, which will be reviewed by Virginia Career Works. **Addiction Recovery** As a member of the [Addiction and Recovery Council](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033804/534051243/534806463?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556), we work together to ensure that people have the resources they need to identify and get help for addiction recovery. Addiction is a life threatening problem for too many Virginians. Please reach out if you need help for yourself or a loved one. The [Substance Use Prevention program](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033805/534051244/1222458286?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556), a partnership between the Fairfax-Falls Church Community Services Board, Fairfax County Public Schools (FCPS) and Juvenile and Domestic Relations District Court's Diversion Unit, works to provide substance use prevention, assessments and treatment to adolescents who are at risk of or who are actively using substances. Youth substance use can be overcome. Early detection and treatment are key to recovery – support is a phone call away. For additional information, please call the Substance Use Disorder Prevention program case manager at 703-866-2121. **Know Your Rights - Resources for Immigrants** I will continue to work to ensure that our communities are welcoming, diverse and provide opportunities for all people to live with dignity and safety. Working together, we can build good lives for our families and our communities. The news of ICE sweeps is distressing. We can help by sharing information with our neighbors regarding their rights. We can volunteer to help people fill out forms and warn people to avoid overpaying for a notary (it can be free and should not be more than $10). We can [print and share the “red cards”](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033806/534051245/-2121718050?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556) which contain directions for handling interaction with law enforcement in several languages. **If you or a loved one encounter immigration enforcement officials, it is essential that you know your rights and have prepared your household for all possible outcomes** : **Ask for a warrant** : The Fourth Amendment of the Constitution protects you from unreasonable search and seizure. You do not have to open your door until you see a valid warrant to enter your home or search your belongings. **You have the right to remain silent** : The Fifth Amendment protects your right to remain silent and not incriminate yourself. You are not required to share any personal information such as your place of birth, immigration status or criminal history. Respectfully say, “I am exercising my right to remain silent.” **Always consult an attorney** : You have a right to speak with an attorney. You do not have to sign anything or hand officials any documents without speaking to an attorney. Try to identify and consult one in advance. Please share this information from the ACLU, available in multiple languages: [“Know Your Rights.”](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033807/534051246/-939671523?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556) We are also working with the Legal Aid Justice Center which has asked that we share their [Rapid Response Toolkit](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/104033808/534051247/1464008473?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmNiNmE0NzctODAwMy1mMDExLTkwY2QtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZmI3IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ca2a69GbjfFm-bd-uQgzODunmR1C2_GHLVFelsLlH5M=&emci=6d313488-7403-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=fcb6a477-8003-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=22486556). **Opportunities** **Free Toolkit for Businesses from Childcare Aware** Supporting working parents isn't just the right thing to do – it’s good for business. By providing resources and child care solutions, you show your team that you care about their well-being, and you'll see the positive results in your company culture and bottom line. clean_body: | | | Boysko Bulletin:Join Me at Our Town Halls 3/18 and 3/24 Dear Peter, It’s March - we recognize women in two different ways. Women’s History Month, observed every March, is a celebration of women’s contributions to history, culture and society and has been observed annually since 1987. and marks the current state of the gender pay gap for women: 83% for full-time, year-round workers and 75% for all workers (including part-time and seasonal). March 25 is the day when women’s pay including the whole prior year’s earnings equals that of what men earned in the prior year. That’s confusing and disturbing - no one should earn less because they are a woman or a minority. The Governor has the opportunity to sign my bill requiring that hiring notices include salary or wage ranges and that salary history isn’t used in hiring - steps that would narrow the wage gap. Please join me for a town hall - this Tuesday in Reston and next Monday in McLean. We will discuss the legislative session and what’s happening with the federal government. There are resources for federal workers and immigrants included in this newsletter. We await the Governor’s signature on my legislation - he has until midnight on March 24th to sign, amend or veto. We will return to Richmond on April 2 to vote on his actions. I look forward to meeting with you soon. Kindest regards, Jennifer B. BoyskoMember, Senate of VirginiaRepresenting the newly created 38th Senate District making up the northernmost portions of Fairfax County (Great Falls, Herndon, McLean, Reston, WolfTrap, parts of unincorporated Vienna and a slice of Falls Church) PS If you would like to support my work, please visit =. Photo Gallery Delegate Sewell and I held a press conference today at a small business, asking the Governor to sign paid family and medical leave legislation. Virginians would have the opportunity to take up to 12 weeks of paid leave in the event of a serious personal illness, to care for a family member with a serious personal illness or to care for a new baby. Recognizing the wonderful volunteers of the Dranesville District Democrats Presenting the impact and importance of federal funding for Virginia's Transportation Infrastructure at the Environmental and Energy Institute's Congressional Camp - prep for the 2026 reauthorization of the federal Surface Transportation Bill Legislative Updates SJ247 Constitutional amendment; fundamental right to reproductive freedom (first reference): Passed the General Assembly! SB1002 Campaign finance; prohibited personal use of campaign funds; complaints, hearings, civil penalty, and advisory opinions: Passed the General Assembly! SB1004 Trailer franchise requirements; applicability to certain trailers : Passed the General Assembly! SB1005 Services for sexual assault patients; provision of information for sexual assault patients; Task Force on Services for Survivors of Sexual Assault; work group; report : Passed the General Assembly! SB1013 Affirmative defense or reduced penalty for a neurocognitive disorder or intellectual or developmental disability : Passed the General Assembly! SB1122 Paid family and medical leave insurance program; notice requirements; civil action : Passed the Senate; failed in the House after it was brought to the Floor as a revenue bill requiring more than a simple majority vote. While frustrating, I am glad that Delegate Sewell's identical bill passed in both the House and the Senate. SB1123 Extreme Weather Relief Act established: passed by in the Senate Agriculture, Conservation and Natural Resources Committee with a letter for further study SB1124 Board of Education; driver education programs; computer-based driver education courses; requirements : Passed the General Assembly! SB1125 Department of Wildlife Resources; propagation of wildlife without a permit unlawful; premature separation; hybridization: Passed the General Assembly! SB1126 Students with individualized education programs or Section 504 Plans; emergency protocol and guide: killed in the full Senate Education and Health Committee although I am pleased that Delegate Cohen’s version passed the General Assembly SB1127 Animal testing facilities; public notification: Passed the General Assembly! SB1129 National Teacher Certification Incentive Reward Program and Fund; eligibility; incentive grant awards: reported from the Senate Education and Health Committee and killed in the Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee. SB1132 Prohibiting employer seeking wage or salary history of prospective employees; wage or salary range transparency; cause of action: Passed the General Assembly! SB1133 Restaurants and catering operations; notice of food containing pork products; civil penalty: Passed the Senate; killed in the House Health Subcommittee of the Committee on Health and Human Services SB1134 Storage of firearms in a residence where a minor or person prohibited from possessing a firearm is present; penalty: Passed the General Assembly! SB1135 Board of Pharmacy; regulation of COMP360: Passed the General Assembly! SB1136 Local anti-rent gouging authority; civil penalty: killed by the Senate General Laws Committee SB1137 Office of Data Governance and Analytics; oversight; Chief Data Officer; duties: Passed the General Assembly! SB1237 Department of Medical Assistance Services; appeals of agency determinations (Admin Bill): Passed the General Assembly! SB1483 Regulated hemp product retail facility registration; fee: passed the Senate; killed by the Agriculture Subcommittee of the House Agriculture, Chesapeake and Natural Resources Committee Also Passed 17 Commendations! Town Halls Coming Soon to Award The Commendations. SJ419 Commending Fellowship SquareSJ420 Commending the Virginia chapter of the American Association of University Women SJ421 Commending Sheila A. OlemSJ422 Commending Namaste ClubSJ423 Commending Spring Hill Elementary SchoolSJ424 Commending Virginia Banton SJ425 Commending Kaiya Amani WilliamsSJ426 Commending Lina LiakakosSJ427 Commending Donielle M. ScherffSJ428 Commending Susy ReyesSJ429 Commending Pradip Dhakal SJ430 Commending Mia MortmanSJ431 Commending Kian Tatsuo LinamSJ432 Commending the Langley High School golf teamSJ433 Commending Renee ShiSJ435 Commending the Langley High School girls' volleyball teamSJ436 Commending John Ulfelder Transportation Updates Coming Soon: Watch the Environmental and Energy Study Institute Congressional Camp Briefing: ) I was on the panel speaking about the importance and impact of federal transportation funding in Virginia. The Surface Transportation bill is reauthorized every five years. Federal funding makes up 17% of . Funding for our highways, bridges, bike and pedestrian paths, and transit all depend on federal funding. For as long as I’ve been serving on the Senate Transportation Committee, the Northern Virginia Transportation Authority, and the Metropolitan Washington Council of Governments Transportation Planning Board, we have had more project proposals than available funding. Virginia is fortunate that we have systems in place to evaluate and weigh projects to ensure the greatest benefit from every transportation dollar spent. | | Constituent Services In 2024, there were more than 28,000 federal jobs in Fairfax County, with 78,800 Fairfax County residents working for the federal government. Serious attempts to move the federal government out of our region and to continue with layoffs will have a significant impact on our economy. Of course, the impacts are most serious for the employees who have been threatened with layoffs or fired. I am so sorry for what is happening. I’ve included resources in this newsletter and you are welcome to email me. Federal workers laid off or furloughed by the new federal administration are encouraged to reach out to their federal legislators - , and your . Fairfax County has created a webpage with a variety of resources that can help former federal employees, contractors and anyone else affected by job loss. Services include searching and preparing for a new job with listings available through the County and a database maintained by the Fairfax County Economic Development Authority. Financial help is available for many health and human service programs, taxes and pet-related expenses. A link to the new webpage is included below: . The Fairfax County Economic Development Authority, with funding from the Fairfax County Board of Supervisors, has introduced a one-stop resource for connecting with hundreds of employers actively hiring in our region: . You can watch their . They have 90,000 jobs posted. For those eligible for unemployment benefits, the Virginia Employment Commission (VEC) requires that people receiving unemployment benefits show proof of job searches each week. I encourage you to seek support for finding a job from . They help identify training programs, job opportunities, and help with resumes, emails, and interviewing skills. My constituents report good results working with VCW. . is a initiative. The site includes suggestions for federal workers seeking to rewrite their resumes for the private sector, plus region-by-region links to major employers’ corporate career pages and links to region-specific job searches on Indeed and LinkedIn. Additional Resources for Federal Employees Facing Unemployment: NOVA New Employment, Exploration and Transition (NOVAnext), is a new program to help recently unemployed federal workers and federal contractors transition to their next careers. The first in the region to provide new, dedicated training funds for unemployed federal workers and federal contractors, NOVA is committing up to $1 million in scholarship aid to allow approved participants to enroll in one of 14 courses at no charge and access NOVA-sponsored online workshops on resume-writing and job search techniques. Many classes prepare students for industry-recognized certifications in specific IT specialties, program management and human resources. To be eligible, federal workers and federal contract workers must have been laid off on or after February 1, 2025, reside within the NOVA service area, meet a residency requirement and fill out an online application, which will be reviewed by Virginia Career Works. For more information, visit If you are a government employee with information related to the politicization of the federal workforce, the firing of civil servants, or waste, fraud, or abuse of taxpayer dollars, you may to the Democrats on the U.S. House Oversight Committee. The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission has resources regarding employment discrimination for federal employees that can be accessed . Employment rights under federal law (U.S. Office of Special Counsel) Whistleblower protections and accompanying resources, click To understand prohibited personnel practices including the right to appeal, visit The Library of Congress Beginner's Guide to Civil Service Employment Law can be found . Help Paying Bills If you are struggling to pay bills, please visit Virginia’s to figure out if you qualify for assistance. You can also reach out to your county - your local government will review your financial situation and connect you with resources. In Fairfax County, contact . are additional resources for food. If you have lost your health insurance, please visit to figure out if you are eligible for Medicaid or for help with Affordable Care Act insurance. If you cannot afford healthcare, visit . Transportation Discounts: 50% Off Metrorail and Metrobus for SNAP Recipients Please share this news - SNAP Recipients qualify for Metro Lift, a 50 percent discount for any travel on Metrorail and Metrobus. Customers in DC, Maryland and Virginia who qualify for the US Department of Agriculture's Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) are eligible to enroll. Easy sign up \- multiple languages. Learn more about reduced fares for seniors 65 and over and those with disabilities . Reach Out for Mental Health Support The is available nationwide. You can also find support for yourself and family members by reaching out to or. The Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration National Helpline: 1-800-662-4357 National Drug Helpline: 1-844-289-0879 National Institute of Mental Health Information Resource Center: 1-866-615-6464 Partnership for Drug-Free Kids: 1-855-378-4373 Addiction Recovery As a member of the , we work together to ensure that people have the resources they need to identify and get help for addiction recovery. Addiction is a life threatening problem for too many Virginians. Please reach out if you need help for yourself or a loved one. The , a partnership between the Fairfax-Falls Church Community Services Board, Fairfax County Public Schools (FCPS) and Juvenile and Domestic Relations District Court's Diversion Unit, works to provide substance use prevention, assessments and treatment to adolescents who are at risk of or who are actively using substances. Youth substance use can be overcome. Early detection and treatment are key to recovery – support is a phone call away. For additional information, please call the Substance Use Disorder Prevention program case manager at 703-866-2121. Know Your Rights - Resources for Immigrants I will continue to work to ensure that our communities are welcoming, diverse and provide opportunities for all people to live with dignity and safety. Working together, we can build good lives for our families and our communities. The news of ICE sweeps is distressing. We can help by sharing information with our neighbors regarding their rights. We can volunteer to help people fill out forms and warn people to avoid overpaying for a notary (it can be free and should not be more than $10). We can which contain directions for handling interaction with law enforcement in several languages. If you or a loved one encounter immigration enforcement officials, it is essential that you know your rights and have prepared your household for all possible outcomes : Ask for a warrant : The Fourth Amendment of the Constitution protects you from unreasonable search and seizure. You do not have to open your door until you see a valid warrant to enter your home or search your belongings. You have the right to remain silent : The Fifth Amendment protects your right to remain silent and not incriminate yourself. You are not required to share any personal information such as your place of birth, immigration status or criminal history. Respectfully say, “I am exercising my right to remain silent.” Always consult an attorney : You have a right to speak with an attorney. You do not have to sign anything or hand officials any documents without speaking to an attorney. Try to identify and consult one in advance. Please share this information from the ACLU, available in multiple languages: We are also working with the Legal Aid Justice Center which has asked that we share their . Additional Resources for immigrants: Fairfax County’s Fairfax County Public Schools: If you are an immigrant in need of legal services, please visit: Call Immigration Rapid Response Hotline, Virginia Network, 855-298-3271 in the event of an ICE raid or arrest For a list of resources provided by the Virginia Department of Social Services, please visit here: Opportunities Free Toolkit for Businesses from Childcare Aware Supporting working parents isn't just the right thing to do – it’s good for business. By providing resources and child care solutions, you show your team that you care about their well-being, and you'll see the positive results in your company culture and bottom line. Take advantage of start building a more supportive workplace for your employees. Give Back is Brain Injury Awareness Month—a time to recognize the resilience of survivors and the vital services that help them rebuild their lives. Brain Injury Services provides free life-changing support to individuals and families affected by brain injury, offering case management, mental health support, vocational assistance, and community programs that foster independence. Our region is facing significant challenges. Recent federal worker layoffs have created economic hardship, not only for our clients but also for caregivers who now face increased uncertainty. The impact of job loss, financial stress, and lack of resources can be devastating for families already navigating the complexities of brain injury recovery. Brain Injury Services offers hope, healing, and a path forward for brain injury survivors and their families. For constituent services and to schedule meetings with your community group, please call (703) 437-0086 or email Senator Boysko@senate.virginia.gov. name: Tobias Read
subject: This headline stopped me dead in my tracks:
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250317em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250317em]
I know it’s been a whirlwind of news ever since President Donald Trump returned to the White House.
But this headline stopped me dead in my tracks, and I wanted to make sure you saw it, too.
Donald Trump just cut millions from the Center for Internet Security, a nonprofit that helps state and local officials run free and fair elections:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250317em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250317em]
FIGHT BACK >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250317em]
Make no mistake: Donald Trump and his MAGA loyalists cut this program because instead of protecting elections, they’d rather just win them — at any cost.
That’s what separates me and my office from the political extremists running the country from DC.
I’m focused on protecting elections, safeguarding democracy, and strengthening voting rights for all Oregonians — no matter their political affiliation.
But Donald Trump doesn’t care about that. All he cares about is winning and holding power — even if that means throwing our democracy under the bus in the process.
That’s not what leadership looks like. And as your Secretary of State, I’m committed to doing everything I can to fight back against partisan extremism like this. But I can’t do it alone, so if you’re in this fight with me, can I count on you to pitch in $25 or more right now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250317em]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250317em]
Thank you,
Tobias Read
Oregon Secretary of State
P.S. Don’t forget! The Oregon Political Tax Credit allows you to donate $50 (if you file single) or $100 (if you file joint) to a candidate, PAC, or party, which will be fully credited back as a rebate — so it’s free to donate! For more information, click here. [https://www.oregon.gov/omd/ONG/Documents/SMFS/Personal%20Financial%20Counselor/Political-Tax-Credit.pdf?]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250317em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250317em]
https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR [https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR] https://twitter.com/TobiasRead [https://twitter.com/TobiasRead] https://instagram.com/tobiasreador [https://instagram.com/tobiasreador]

Friends of Tobias Read (5208)
PO Box 42307
Portland, OR 97242
United States

name: Sarah McBride
subject: Republicans are a bunch of overgrown schoolyard bullies
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Our constituents shouldn’t have to suffer because Republicans want to act like children.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250317-1&t=1&akid=1866%2E587540%2E_J0yah)
Hi Peter, it’s Congresswoman Sarah McBride. Leaders We Deserve said I could reach out because you have what it takes to stand up in critical moments — and this is one of them.
**Yet another GOP politician decided to get his own 15 Minutes of Twitter Fame at my expense.**
At a subcommittee hearing, the Republican chairman referred to me as “Mr. McBride.” And when Rep. Bill Keating defended me, the chair _adjourned the hearing_ to avoid acknowledging his own immaturity.
**Republicans are a bunch of overgrown schoolyard bullies.**
Our constituents shouldn’t have to suffer because Republicans want to act like children. **This pettiness needs to end.** I was there ready to ask questions on nuclear non-proliferation and US support for our democratic allies in Europe.
**I ran for Congress to roll up my sleeves and do the job.** No matter what they throw at me, I’m 100% focused on delivering for Delawareans. And if that means listening to their childish insults, _so be it._
**[Peter, I’m proud to have Leaders We Deserve’s support at this moment, but we need you with us. Can you split an urgent $5 contribution to help us cut through the noise and keep up the fight for all of us?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20250317-1?refcode=em20250317-1866&amount=5&refcode2=1866_587540__J0yah&akid=1866%2E587540%2E_J0yah)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20250317-1?amount=5&refcode=em20250317-1866&refcode2=1866_587540__J0yah&akid=1866%2E587540%2E_J0yah)**
Thank you for your support,
Sarah McBride
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20250317-1?refcode=em20250317-1866&amount=5&refcode2=1866_587540__J0yah&akid=1866%2E587540%2E_J0yah)
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20250317-1&t=2&refcode2=1866_587540__J0yah&akid=1866%2E587540%2E_J0yah)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=1866%2E587540%2E_J0yah)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=1866%2E587540%2E_J0yah)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/big-oils-last-ditch-effort-immunity?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Most Americans don't realize that scientists who worked for Big Oil companies like ExxonMobil warned more than half a century ago that continued exploitation of fossil fuels _will_ cause climate catastrophe.
Big Oil knew this to be true, but lied anyway. But it gets even worse. And if we don't act now, we'll suffer the continued consequences of Big Oil's climate destruction. **Let 's Address This.**
**The fight to stave off corporate corruption isn 't easy. I cannot do it alone. Subscribe below and join the movement.**
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..CCUGHGrSKApRyA7dttArJAQv54HnDvWaV3NqokpaL-Y?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=159285147)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8e9c093d-3bcc-4bf8-beee-3ec6bf784b87?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Credit: Fossil Free Memo
## **Fact: Fossil Fuel Companies Knowingly Destroyed Our Planet**
While fossil fuel companies like ExxonMobil, Shell, BP, and Chevron lied about the climate crisis, they reaped stupendous record profits to the tune of trillions of dollars. And it still gets worse.
Big Oil reaped these profits while communities nationally and globally suffered the consequences of their deception. Studies show that these catastrophes disproportionately harm low-income people and Black and Latino communities, demonstrating the racial injustice resulting from climate injustice. And yes, it still gets even worse.
After knowingly lying to the public, making record profits off that lie, and destroying countless lives and communities, now Big Oil wants legal immunity. Yep, they want a get-out-of-jail-free card that would shield them from lawsuits and financial responsibility for the damage they've knowingly caused. And unless we act now, Big Oil just might get away with it.
This isn't speculation. It's happening right now. In Maryland, fossil fuel industry lobbyists are pushing legislation that would grant blanket immunity to oil companies, preventing them from being sued for climate-related destruction. Meanwhile, the American Petroleum Institute and other industry groups are filing lawsuits against climate superfund laws, setting the stage for congressional intervention.
Donald Trump himself has promised to "stop the wave of frivolous litigation from environmental extremists," making it clear that Big Oil has a powerful ally in its fight to evade justice. If they succeed, it won't just be about climate lawsuits--this will set a precedent for corporations and government entities to dodge accountability across industries.
Again, this isn't speculation. It is already happening. Just last week the Supreme Court ruled 5-4 in favor of the city of San Francisco, allowing them to overrule the EPA, ignore the Clean Water Act, and discharge feces-filled sewage water into "receiving bodies."
So yes, things are pretty bad. But what can we do to make things better? It starts by following a proven model to protect the human rights we all have.
## **The Corporate Playbook: Protect Profits, Not People**
If this Big Oil strategy sounds familiar, that's because it is. Industries that have caused massive harm always try to escape liability when public pressure mounts. For example:
* The Gun Industry's Immunity Shield: The Protection of Lawful Commerce in Arms Act (PLCAA) protects gun manufacturers from lawsuits, even when their products fuel mass shootings. Imagine if families of Sandy Hook victims could sue Remington and win in court--but were barred from doing so? That's exactly what Big Oil wants for themselves.
* The Tobacco and Opioid Industries Were Held Accountable: Unlike the gun industry, Big Tobacco and opioid manufacturers were forced to pay billions in settlements for their role in lying about the dangers of their products. Now, fossil fuel companies want to ensure they never have to pay for the fires, floods, and extreme weather they helped create.

Remember, at its core, this is a justice issue. When corporations evade accountability, the burden of climate destruction falls on us--taxpayers, working-class people, and especially frontline communities already suffering the most. Here's what happens when polluters don't pay:
✅ Taxpayers foot the bill for disaster recovery, infrastructure repairs, and medical costs
✅ Vulnerable communities bear the worst impacts, despite contributing the least to climate change
✅ The cycle of destruction continues, as corporations face no consequences and continue profiting from climate devastation
The above examples of the firearm and tobacco industries prove that when we work together, we can hold powerful corporations and industries accountable. We must do the same with Big Oil. If we fail, and instead allow fossil fuel companies to rewrite the rules and dodge accountability, who's next? Will corporations responsible for water contamination, unsafe working conditions, or dangerous pharmaceuticals demand the same immunity? The fight against Big Oil is a fight to stop corporate lawlessness before it's too late.
Here is how to act.
## **The Time to Act is Now**
Established organizations like Make Polluters Pay are fighting to force fossil fuel companies to cover the costs of the damage they've caused--through climate superfund legislation and climate liability lawsuits. We've done this before with Big Tobacco and Big Pharma. It's time to do it again with Big Oil.
The Make Polluters Pay Plan would establish a Polluters Pay Climate Fund, funded by contributions from major oil and gas companies like ExxonMobil, Chevron, and Shell. The amount each company pays into the fund would be based on their share of historic emissions.
If Trump and Big Oil succeed in securing immunity, not only is there no fund to hold them accountable, it will be a disaster for climate accountability--and for democracy itself. We cannot allow an unelected industry of polluters to override the courts and the will of the people.
**Here 's what you can do right now:**
1. **Raise your voice.** Take a moment to sign the petition to demand accountability and block fossil fuel immunity.
[Petition to Block Fossil Fuel Immunity](https://substack.com/redirect/d498bd77-51ae-43b3-b301-9fa51c386211?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
2. **Subscribe to Make Polluters Pay on Substack at Fossil Free Memo.** This will help you to stay informed and take action. It is critical we elevate the organizations who are the tip of the spear in fighting for climate justice.

| [Fossil Free Memo](https://substack.com/redirect/817424e5-a40c-4adf-aa21-7f7f08656829?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[Talking points for the fight against fossil fuels](https://substack.com/redirect/817424e5-a40c-4adf-aa21-7f7f08656829?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[By Fossil Free Media](https://substack.com/redirect/817424e5-a40c-4adf-aa21-7f7f08656829?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---|---
3. **Share this post.** The more people who know what's happening, the harder it will be for Big Oil to operate in secrecy. We the People fought back against the firearm industry and big tobacco by publicizing our stories and making them heard. We must do the same with Big Oil.

## **Conclusion**
Big Oil is counting on public apathy and political corruption to secure its get-out-of-jail-free card. But history proves that when people unite against corporate lawlessness, justice prevails. We've held Big Tobacco, the gun industry, and opioid manufacturers accountable--we can and must do the same with fossil fuel companies. The time to act is now. Sign the petition, stay informed, and spread the word. If we let Big Oil win, we set a precedent for unchecked corporate destruction across industries. But if we fight back and make polluters pay, we can protect our planet, our communities, and our future. Corporations don't stop unless we make them. Let's make them.
**Before you go! More than 123,000 activists are subscribed to** _**Let 's Address This.** _**We are doing everything in our power to stave off fascism in the United States. Subscribe and join us.**

| | Peter, This week, Governor Sanders visited Arkansans affected by storm damage, celebrated the passing of educational reform legislation, and highlighted new savings and improvements to government efficiency under Arkansas Forward.
---
|

| | ♥️ **GOVERNOR SANDERS VISITED ARKANSANS IMPACTED BY THE DEVASTATING STORMS THIS WEEK:**_“In the worst moments, you see the best of Arkansans. Showing up and helping one another.”_ _[**HelpArkansas.com**](http://links.shsforgov.com/s/c/hReEb02gpyKJxu5Byqr8lJz-2s3PvSnkh87ZU4LIsARdf_XO1qCxnspPbkKW96iSwZrJkQSSg1CNdqBS9auA8J4wsts7kDplgCFlaOqmsW9GAIeo8B6g_AZfbLOzQjfnpJLlSqEM8mtd1LCuwWC87FUeUTHEVfWxHPHBWYprhczghbRjoIR4d_OyCd10Qj4uqj9gE32NJ_MA27eBpnueGOMuhjNL-NAD4tya00MYL14sroFSlIkmZY3UAfUFdXgF/LpfIpNrsKmMkUP33YMXVMn5pUbMz9D66/7)_

name: The Democratic National Committee
subject: Here are three things you can do right now to help:
body:
---
Derek — Since Trump’s second term began we’ve received a lot of feedback from our supporters, like this message from Declan: _“I would love a call to action that wasn’t always primarily tied to giving money. I am frustrated and disheartened.”_ And we understand. We feel that too. That’s why today we wanted to share a couple of calls to action: things you can do right now to get involved, help Democrats fight back, and lay the long-term groundwork we’ll need to win elections this year, next year, and beyond. We know it can be easy to feel powerless with all the chaos going on. But that’s what Trump and his allies want. They want to see us check out and let them do whatever they want without a fight. We can’t allow that to happen. There’s too much at stake. **_So here are three things you can do right now to help:_**
1. We have crucial elections in Florida and Wisconsin in just a few weeks. In Florida, Democrats have the opportunity to win _two_ previously Republican-held seats. In Wisconsin, as Elon Musk pours millions of dollars to support the right-wing candidate, an election that will determine control of the state Supreme Court offers an opportunity to fight Donald Trump and Musk’s agenda. The stakes couldn’t be higher. You can get involved from wherever you live by [clicking this link.](https://ak.democrats.org/go/14282?refcode=em_20250317_2989_fr&t=2&akid=2989%2E22930333%2E92a01c)
2. You may have seen that Republicans are currently trying to push through a giant tax cut for billionaires like Elon Musk at the expense of everyone else. If you have a Republican senator, [call them and tell them to say NO to the Trump tax scam.](http://ak.democrats.org/go/14341?refcode=em_20250317_2989_fr&t=3&akid=2989%2E22930333%2E92a01c)
3. [Attend a town hall near you](https://ak.democrats.org/go/14352?refcode=em_20250317_2989_fr&t=4&akid=2989%2E22930333%2E92a01c) to connect with neighbors, community leaders, and organizers to discuss the issues that matter most.

**[VOLUNTEER](https://ak.democrats.org/go/14282?refcode=em_20250317_2989_fr&t=5&akid=2989%2E22930333%2E92a01c)** **[CALL YOUR SENATOR](http://ak.democrats.org/go/14341?refcode=em_20250317_2989_fr&t=6&akid=2989%2E22930333%2E92a01c)** **[ATTEND A TOWN HALL](https://ak.democrats.org/go/14352?refcode=em_20250317_2989_fr&t=7&akid=2989%2E22930333%2E92a01c)** We hope you’ll consider getting involved and we will continue to share opportunities regularly. Thank you for your support, The Democrats

**Your support helps us continue to build a strong Democratic Party that can fight back against Trump’s billionaire allies and elect Democrats up and down the ballot who will fight for working families.** [If this work is important to you, too, we hope you’ll consider chipping in $3 to help fund it.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-march-2025/?refcode=em_20250317_2989_fr&amount=3&refcode2=2989_22930333_92a01c&akid=2989%2E22930333%2E92a01c)

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

| Peter, remember back in January when Donald Trump shut down the _White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention_ just HOURS after taking office? Well now, the far right’s assault on gun safety is even closer to home... **Governor DeSantis is working to repeal Florida’s Red Flag Law.** This bipartisan legislation was passed in 2018 after the Parkland shooting. It raised the legal minimum age to purchase a gun, added waiting periods, and allowed courts to seize weapons from dangerous individuals. And as a former organizer with _March for Our Lives,_ which was founded as a response to the Parkland shooting, this law was the direct product of our advocacy. But now DeSantis is putting his extreme agenda ahead of our safety, and I refuse to let him take us back. My work didn’t end with March for Our Lives. I’m still fighting in Congress, introducing legislation like the Office of Gun Violence Prevention Act and organizing year round to amplify the voices of communities impacted the most. But I need you to help me stay here. [**_Peter, the gun lobby knows that I’m one of their fiercest opponents. That’s why in no time, they’ll be funding challengers to unseat me. clean_body: | ~~ | Peter, remember back in January when Donald Trump shut down the White House Office of Gun Violence Prevention just HOURS after taking office? Well now, the far right’s assault on gun safety is even closer to home... Governor DeSantis is working to repeal Florida’s Red Flag Law. This bipartisan legislation was passed in 2018 after the Parkland shooting. It raised the legal minimum age to purchase a gun, added waiting periods, and allowed courts to seize weapons from dangerous individuals. And as a former organizer with March for Our Lives, which was founded as a response to the Parkland shooting, this law was the direct product of our advocacy. But now DeSantis is putting his extreme agenda ahead of our safety, and I refuse to let him take us back. My work didn’t end with March for Our Lives. I’m still fighting in Congress, introducing legislation like the Office of Gun Violence Prevention Act and organizing year round to amplify the voices of communities impacted the most. name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: "We don't have the money"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
Where's all the rest of it going?
#
I actually believe that people should age with dignity in this country.

I believe that people should have access to healthcare.
Before people say "We don't have the money" for it:
We “don't have the money” for a better social safety net because **we let *ssholes like Elon Musk gut Medicaid and Social Security in order for him to pay for _his own_ tax cuts** and _his own_ billion dollars in private federal contracts.
This guy takes billions and billions of _your_ tax dollars, of _your_ public funds — and it's not just Elon.
A lot of these “tech guys” that were standing behind Trump at the inauguration talk about themselves as “tech,” but **they're really just defense contractors**.
They're the new Boeing. They're the new Lockheed Martin.
They are robbing us of the tiny slivers of what we have left in our collective public social safety net in order to get billion dollar defense contracts.
Because here’s the thing: you could fire every single federal worker, and it would still be only 4% of the federal budget.
**Where's all the rest of it going?**
If you actually wanted to go after 'waste,' fraud, and abuse, you will go after defense contractors.
But the problem is those are the guys who are making the money off of cuts to Medicaid, Medicare, and Social Security.
The Trump trifecta has taken hold, along with their billionaire donors.
Our movement for working families and everyday people will have to fight harder than ever these next four years.
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250317-2.PVdRgK&refcode=em20250317-8370-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8370%2E2526796%2E3aUIrK)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250317-2.PVdRgK&refcode=em20250317-8370-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=8370%2E2526796%2E3aUIrK)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250317-2?refcode=em20250317-8370-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=8370_2526796_3aUIrK&akid=8370%2E2526796%2E3aUIrK)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250317-8370-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=8370%2E2526796%2E3aUIrK)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=23&akid=8370%2E2526796%2E3aUIrK) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=24&akid=8370%2E2526796%2E3aUIrK) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=25&akid=8370%2E2526796%2E3aUIrK)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=8370%2E2526796%2E3aUIrK)

name: Pat Herrity
subject: Thank You!
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Friends of Pat Herrity
body:
|
---
| |
---
| |
Peter,

I'm excited to announce that this morning, we filed the signatures of Virginia voters required for my name to appear on the ballot! Thanks to your hard work over the last several months, we turned in more than 15,000 signatures from across the Commonwealth.

Our campaign was the only one in the Lieutenant Governor's race to file our required signatures when filing opened this morning, and that means that my name will be listed first on the Lieutenant Governor primary ballot if additional candidates do file the required signatures.

This would not have been possible without countless hours put in by volunteers in every corner of Virginia - I am tremendously grateful for the support we've received and excited for what comes next.

Now that we're on the ballot, it's time to turn to the real work of securing a resounding victory in November. As soon as I'm recovered and ready to travel, we'll be back to barnstorming the state - campaigning to the voters we need to secure statewide victory and continue the commonsense, conservative policies under which Virginia thrives.

Stay tuned for how you can help, and I look forward to seeing you out there soon!

Respectfully,
---
| |
---
---
|
---
**Pat Herrity**
Springfield District Supervisor, Fairfax County
Candidate for Lieutenant Governor

clean_body: | | | | | Peter, I'm excited to announce that this morning, we filed the signatures of Virginia voters required for my name to appear on the ballot! Thanks to your hard work over the last several months, we turned in more than 15,000 signatures from across the Commonwealth. Our campaign was the only one in the Lieutenant Governor's race to file our required signatures when filing opened this morning, and that means that my name will be listed first on the Lieutenant Governor primary ballot if additional candidates do file the required signatures. This would not have been possible without countless hours put in by volunteers in every corner of Virginia - I am tremendously grateful for the support we've received and excited for what comes next. Now that we're on the ballot, it's time to turn to the real work of securing a resounding victory in November. As soon as I'm recovered and ready to travel, we'll be back to barnstorming the state - campaigning to the voters we need to secure statewide victory and continue the commonsense, conservative policies under which Virginia thrives. Stay tuned for how you can help, and I look forward to seeing you out there soon! Respectfully, | | | Pat Herrity Springfield District Supervisor, Fairfax County Candidate for Lieutenant Governor | | | | | | | | | | | www.patforva.com | | Paid for and authorized by Friends of Pat Herrity | | | Copyright © 2025 Friends of Pat Herrity, all rights reserved. Our mailing address is: Friends of Pat HerrityP.O Box 2863Springfield, VA 22152 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or
